name: Tales of the Valiant Monster Vault

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The aboleth is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The aboleth can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the aboleth fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Probing Telepathy
entries: If a creature the aboleth can see communicates telepathically with the aboleth, the aboleth learns the creature’s greatest desires.

trait.name: Slime Pox
entries: Characterized by the slimy pustules that erupt across the victim’s body, slime pox is a disease that infects creatures attacked by an aboleth or that come into contact with an aboleth’s slime. Until the disease is cured, the infected creature can breathe air and water, can’t regain HP except when underwater, and takes 6 (1d12) acid damage for every 1 hour it remains outside of water or without moisture applied to its skin. Every 24 hours that elapse, the infected creature must make a DC 16 CHA save. Each time a creature fails this save, it views the infecting aboleth more and more favorably, becoming the aboleth’s doting and protective thrall after three failed saves. The successes and failures don’t need to be consecutive, but once a creature has acquired three of a kind, it no longer has to make this save, resulting in either the creature becoming a thrall of the aboleth or an autonomous—though infected—creature. Slime pox can be cured only by the heal spell or similar magic.

trait.name: Slimy Skin
entries: The aboleth is coated in an otherworldly mucus. A creature that touches the aboleth or that hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be infected with slime pox (see the Slime Pox trait).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The aboleth makes three Tentacle or Psychic Bolt attacks. If two Tentacle attacks hit one target, the target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or contract the slime pox disease (see the Slime Pox trait).

action.name: Tentacle
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Psychic Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +8 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) psychic damage.

action.name: Psychic Torrent (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The aboleth floods the minds of up to three creatures it can see within 60 feet of it with random and horrific memories from its eons-long life. Each target must make a DC 16 WIS save. On a failure, a creature takes 49 (14d6) psychic damage and is incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as its mind reels from the torrent of images, thoughts, and memories. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t incapacitated.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The aboleth can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The aboleth regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The aboleth automatically detects every creature and trap within 60 feet of it, pinpointing the current location of each.

legendary.name: Swim
entries: The aboleth swims up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Slime Drain (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: One creature infected with slime pox within 30 feet of the aboleth takes 10 (3d6) acid damage, and the aboleth regains HP equal to the damage the creature takes. Angel, Deva Bright white wings spread from the shoulders of this powerfully built, lithe human. The angel holds an ornate golden mace in one hand, and a halo of light surrounds its head.

legendary.name: Celestial Emissaries
entries: Devas carry messages of peace from divine powers to mortals. They transform themselves into benign humanoids and animals, so their appearances don’t raise alarm.

legendary.name: Mortal Concerns
entries: Because of the time devas spend among humanoids, they often become involved in humanoid lives. They enter battle alongside their adopted friends, provided the cause is just and good. Devas also provide healing and other care for the wounded and infirm.

legendary.name: Mace Obsessed
entries: A deva exclusively wields a mace. A deva will reluctantly use another weapon if it loses its mace, but it shows less enthusiasm for battle when bereft of the weapon. Devas enjoy baroque flanged, engraved, and inlaid versions of these weapons and often learn smithing so they can produce their own. Each deva’s mace is unique and more closely identifies the individual angel than its name.

trait.name: Angelic Resilience
entries: The deva is resistant to radiant damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed and frightened conditions.

trait.name: Angelic Senses
entries: The deva can pinpoint the location of Aberrations, Celestials, Fiends, and Undead within 30 feet of it and knows if a place or object it can sense is consecrated or desecrated.

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: The deva’s weapon attacks are magical. When the deva hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4d8 radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The deva doesn’t require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The deva has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Reviving Prayer
entries: The deva spends 10 minutes praying to its deity for the safe return of the soul of a slain creature. At the end of the prayer, the deva touches a creature that has been dead no more than 24 hours and returns the creature to life with a number of HP equal to half its HP maximum. Reviving Prayer can’t return to life a creature that has died of old age, nor can it restore any missing body parts. A creature restored to life in this way can’t be returned to life by this deva again for 30 days.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The deva makes three Mace attacks.

action.name: Mace
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 18 (4d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Healing Touch (3/Day)
entries: The deva touches another creature. The target magically regains 20 (4d8 + 2) HP and is freed from any curse, disease, poison, blindness, or deafness.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The deva magically transforms into a Large or smaller Beast or Humanoid that has a challenge rating no higher than its own, or back into its true form, which is Celestial. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it or is borne by the new form (the deva’s choice). It reverts to its true form if it dies. In a new form, the deva retains its Angelic Resilience, Angelic Senses, and Immortal Nature traits and its HP , HD, ability to speak, proficiencies, and INT, WIS, and CHA scores, as well as this bonus action. Its statistics and capabilities are otherwise replaced by those of the new form, except any class features or legendary actions of that form. Angel, Planetar This green-skinned human with golden wings bears a massive sword. A halo of golden light hovers above its head.

bonus.name: Battlefield Commanders
entries: Planetars engage in never-ending battle against the scourge of the Lower Planes, and they serve as field commanders who report to solars. They often lead from the front, using their powerful blades to cut down their foes. Though they value those under their command, they prioritize destroying their foes over healing their soldiers.

bonus.name: Angelic Envoys
entries: When goodly deities wish to broker a truce, they command planetars to break from battle to oversee discussions—which they treat with as much fervor and earnestness as they treat blood and battle. Their instinctive ability to discover untruths makes them ideal negotiators, and their ability to see things as they truly are makes it nigh impossible for an assassin to target attendees forging a treaty.

bonus.name: Disliked by Mortals
entries: Planetars don’t withhold their judgment when someone utters even the smallest of untruths, leading many mortals to keep clear of them or keep quiet in their presence to avoid awkward situations.

trait.name: Angelic Resilience
entries: The planetar is resistant to radiant damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed and frightened conditions.

trait.name: Angelic Senses
entries: The planetar can pinpoint the location of Aberrations, Celestials, Fiends, and Undead within 30 feet of it and knows if a place or object it can sense is consecrated or desecrated.

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: The planetar’s weapon attacks are magical. When it hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 7d8 radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Divine Awareness
entries: The planetar knows if it hears a lie.

trait.name: Divine Protection
entries: Aberrations, Celestials, Fiends, and Undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against the planetar.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The planetar doesn’t require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The planetar has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The planetar makes two Greatsword attacks, or it makes three Radiant Bolt attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting. If two Greatsword or Radiant Bolt attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 19 CON save or be blinded until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (4d6 + 7) slashing damage plus 31 (7d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Radiant Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +11 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 33 (6d8 + 6) radiant damage.

action.name: Radiant Burst (Recharge 6)
entries: A pulse of divine power ripples out from the planetar’s golden halo. Each hostile creature within 15 feet of the planetar must make a DC 19 DEX save, taking 56 (16d6) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Each friendly creature within 15 feet of the planetar is empowered with divine energy and has advantage on all melee attack rolls it makes before the start of the planetar’s next turn.

action.name: Healing Touch (4/Day)
entries: The planetar touches another creature. The target magically regains 30 (6d8 + 3) HP and is freed from any curse, disease, poison, blindness, or deafness.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The planetar casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 19).
At will: command, invisibility (self only)
3/day each: daylight, raise dead (as an action)
Angel, Solar A gigantic sword hovers in place near this golden human adorned with golden wings infused with an inner light. White-hot light emanates from its eyes.

action.name: Heavenly Generals
entries: Solars command the legions of heaven at every major battle against demon lords, archdevils, and other major powers of the Lower Planes. Though they strive to eradicate every major fiend, they typically succeed only in preventing the fiends from spreading far beyond their planar bastions. Genius tacticians and strategists, solars give orders to planetars and others and keep themselves in reserve. However, if given an opportunity to strike down a major foe, a solar takes to the skies with its strongest celestial warband and carves a path through the battlefield. They reserve their most powerful attacks for their equals in combat, but they have the strength to bring an entire host of angels to their ultimate opponents.

action.name: Frightening to Mortals
entries: As one of the most powerful of the host of angels, solars are utterly alien to creatures unaccustomed to seeing them. Their voices boom, they seem to tower over those who even match them in height, and they don’t hesitate to smite someone who stands in the way of their objectives. Animals, beasts, and most humanoids are often terrified by the mere presence of a solar. A SOLAR’S LAIR A solar’s lair is typically a command outpost on a battlefield, a tower overlooking fiendish lands, or other similar bastion of battle. A solar can turn any tent, outpost, supply wagon, or similar battlefield position into its lair as long as it holds the position for at least 24 hours, and the lair extends up to 100 feet in all directions from that position. The solar has access to its lair actions as long as it remains within the area of its lair. When encountered in its lair, the solar has a challenge rating of 22. LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the solar can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the solar can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Dancing Weapons. Weapons of light in the shape of

swords, axes, maces, and similar objects appear around up to three creatures the solar can see within 120 feet of it. The solar makes one ranged attack roll (+13 to hit) against each target. On a hit, the target takes 14 (4d6) radiant damage.
• Divine Echoes. The sounds of trumpets, horns, drums,

and other musical instruments of battle resound within the lair. Each hostile creature the solar can see within 60 feet of it must succeed on a DC 22 CON save or take 7 (2d6) thunder damage. Each friendly creature the solar can see within 60 feet of it regains 7 (2d6) HP . Fiends and Undead that fail this save are vulnerable to radiant damage until initiative count 20 on the next round.
• Erase Shadows. Each object within 60 feet of the solar

that isn’t being worn or carried sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet until initiative count 20 on the next round. This light is sunlight. Each hostile creature in this light must succeed on a DC 22 DEX save or take 7 (2d6) radiant damage, be blinded until the end of its next turn, and have disadvantage on DEX (Stealth) checks while in the light. REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a solar’s lair is suffused with its blazing righteousness, which creates one or more of the following effects:
• Calming Presence. Wild Beasts within 5 miles of the

lair aren’t hostile unless attacked, and domestic Beasts, such as horses and oxen, within 5 miles of the lair are easier to handle, granting creatures advantage on WIS (Animal Handling) checks when interacting with the Beasts.
• Domain of Justice. Within 3 miles of the lair, creatures

have disadvantage on CHA (Deception) checks. In addition, when a creature with an INT of 6 or higher attacks an unarmed creature within 3 miles of the lair, the attacker must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or the attack fails.
• Tactician’s Gaze. While in its lair, the solar can choose

to see and hear through creatures within 1 mile of its lair that have divine power or a divine connection of some kind, such as Celestials, clerics, paladins, aasimars, and similar creatures, and the solar can telepathically communicate with such a creature. If the solar dies, these effects end immediately. If the solar leaves the area and establishes a new lair elsewhere, these effects fade in 1d6 days.

trait.name: Angelic Resilience
entries: The solar is resistant to radiant damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed and frightened conditions.

trait.name: Angelic Senses
entries: The solar can pinpoint the location of Aberrations, Celestials, Fiends, and Undead within 30 feet of it and knows if a place or object it can sense is consecrated or desecrated.

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: The solar’s weapon attacks are magical. When the solar hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 6d8 radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Divine Awareness
entries: The solar knows if it hears a lie.

trait.name: Divine Protection
entries: Aberrations, Celestials, Fiends, and Undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against the solar.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The solar doesn’t require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the solar fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The solar has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The solar makes three Greatsword or Holy Fire Bolt attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting. If two Greatsword or Holy Fire Bolt attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 22 CON save or be blinded until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 8) slashing damage plus 27 (6d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Holy Fire Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +14 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 34 (6d8 + 7) radiant damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage.

action.name: Healing Touch (4/Day)
entries: The solar touches another creature. The target magically regains 40 (8d8 + 4) HP and is freed from any curse, disease, poison, blindness, or deafness.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The solar casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using WIS as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 22).
At will: command, invisibility (self only)
3/day each: greater restoration, resurrection (as an action)
1/day: holy aura

bonus.name: Celestial Tactics
entries: The solar uses one of the following tactics:
• Empower Strikes. The solar sends divine power into

the weapons of up to two friendly creatures it can see within 30 feet of it. Each target has advantage on the next weapon attack roll it makes before the start of the solar’s next turn. If that attack hits, the weapon deals an extra 7 (2d6) radiant damage.
• Inspire Resolve. One friendly creature the solar can

see within 30 feet of it gains 14 (4d6) temporary HP and becomes immune to the charmed and frightened conditions until the start of the solar’s next turn, ending any effect currently causing the target to be charmed or frightened.
• Manipulate Battlefield. The solar commands up to three

friendly creatures it can see to reposition themselves on the battlefield. Each target can use a reaction to move up to half its speed in a direction of the solar’s choice. This movement is unaffected by difficult terrain and doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The solar can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The solar regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Teleport
entries: The solar magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

legendary.name: Searing Burst (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The solar emits divine energy. Each creature of its choice within 10 feet of it must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) fire damage and 14 (4d6) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Intimidating Glare (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The solar glares at up to three creatures it can see within 30 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 22 CHA save or be cowed until the end of its next turn. While cowed, a creature’s speed is halved, and it can’t look at the solar. Animated Objects The emptiness within the suit of plate mail’s visor echoes a bit; no creature controls the armor from inside it. An iron sword floats in midair at rest beside it—then suddenly swings and flourishes as though wielded by a trained warrior.

legendary.name: Living Objects
entries: Animated objects are nonmagical objects given life by a spellcaster. Though little more than their mundane counterparts, animated objects can be given basic instructions to follow—they do so indefinitely or until given new instructions by their creators. The most common animated objects are decorative or seemingly innocuous armor, weapons, and rugs instructed to protect their creators’ homes, laboratories, or treasuries.

legendary.name: Animated Armor
entries: Whether it is field armor with dents and scratches from battle, or ornately decorated for a pleasing display, animated armor moves as quickly as those who might wear it. It pummels foes with its gauntlets, and it can kick or ram opponents if its gauntlets are missing.

legendary.name: Flying Sword
entries: Often paired with animated armor, a flying sword can be equally battle-worn or ornate. It may also sit alone, among a rack of weapons, ready to fly out at intruders. Though more maneuverable than most animated objects, its fragility makes it easier to destroy.

legendary.name: Rug of Smothering
entries: One of the most insidious animated objects, the rug of smothering usually waits until a victim draws near before attempting to grab and suffocate it. Beyond the immediate danger of constriction and suffocation, the rug also passes on some of the damage inflicted on it by the victim’s allies. Despite its name, many large textiles can be animated as rugs of smothering, such as tapestries, blankets, or even heraldic flags and banners.

monster.name: Animated Armor
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The armor is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by dispel magic, the armor must succeed on a CON save against the caster’s spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The armor doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The armor is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the armor remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal suit of armor.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The animated armor makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Helmet Bash (19 HP or Fewer)
entries: The animated armor slams its helmet into a creature it can sense within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or take 5 (2d4) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone.

monster.name: Flying Sword
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The sword is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by dispel magic, the sword must succeed on a CON save against the caster’s spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The sword doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The sword is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the sword remains motionless and isn’t flying, it is indistinguishable from a normal sword.

action.name: Slash
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Whirling Blade (6 HP or Fewer)
entries: The flying sword makes a Slash attack against a target it can sense within 5 feet of it.

monster.name: Rug of Smothering
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The rug is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by dispel magic, the rug must succeed on a CON save against the caster’s spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The rug doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The rug is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Damage Transfer
entries: While it is grappling a creature, the rug takes only half the damage dealt to it (rounded down), and the creature grappled by the rug takes the other half.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the rug remains motionless and isn’t flying, it is indistinguishable from a normal rug.

action.name: Smother
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one Medium or smaller creature. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 13). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, blinded, and unable to breathe, and the rug can’t fly and can’t Smother another target.

action.name: Squeeze Prey
entries: The rug of smothering tightens around a creature it is grappling. The target must make a DC 13 STR save, taking 18 (4d8) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A breathing creature that fails this save by 5 or more has the remaining air squeezed from its lungs and begins suffocating.

bonus.name: Rolling Charge (25 HP or Fewer)
entries: If it isn’t grappling a creature, the rug curls up and rolls along the ground up to triple its speed straight toward a creature it can sense. If it stops within 5 feet of the target, it can make one Smother attack against the target. Ankheg Chittering escapes the sharp mandibles of this massive green and yellow insect. Liquid drips from its mandibles, sizzling as it hits the ground.

bonus.name: Terrors of the Plains
entries: Ankhegs nest just below the surface of grasslands or scrub forests, waiting to ambush prey on the ground above. Their preferred meals are cattle, sheep, horses, and game animals, but they eat anything they can overpower.

bonus.name: Deadly Digestive Fluid
entries: The insectoids constantly generate stomach acid which flows to their mandibles, coating them and making the ankheg’s bites considerably more dangerous than those of most large insects. Ankhegs can also contract their thoracic diaphragms to expel a line of acid, emptying out their stomachs in the process. Ankhegs often use this when retreating from stout opposition.

bonus.name: Gripping Mandibles
entries: Ankhegs have powerful mandibles that can lock into place to better hold struggling prey. Once prey is subdued, ankhegs dig their way out of danger to enjoy their food in peace.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The ankheg is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) acid damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 13). Until this grapple ends, the ankheg can’t Bite another target.

action.name: Acid Spray (Recharge 6)
entries: The ankheg spits acid in a line that is 30 feet long and 5 feet wide, provided it has no creature grappled. Each creature in that line must make a DC 13 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Save for Later
entries: The ankheg quickly digs a small hole and stuffs one creature grappled by it into the hole, burying the creature and ending the grapple. A buried creature is restrained and unable to breathe or stand up. A creature, including the buried creature, can take its action to free the buried creature by succeeding on a DC 13 STR check. Astral Destroyer A massive, serpentine creature with a multipronged tail and four claws flies through the astral sea on two pairs of spiked, black wings.

bonus.name: World Eater
entries: Astral destroyers roam the Astral and Ethereal planes, and they hop into the Material Plane to feast. They are the death of any land where they alight, as they tear the fabric of the world into pieces. As hungry, instinct-driven creatures, astral destroyers can’t be reasoned with and are nearly impossible to stop.

bonus.name: Magical Delicacies
entries: Astral destroyers consider any source of magic particularly delicious, and clever heroes might lure an astral destroyer away from a village in danger by presenting it with a magical meal. The astral destroyer’s bite can strip magic from items, and many worlds have lost their magic or had it severely disrupted by a gathering of ravenous astral destroyers.

monster.name: Astral Destroyer
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The astral destroyer is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Astral Traveler
entries: The astral destroyer doesn’t require ambient pressure and can survive up to 7 days without air before it risks suffocating. While traveling in the Astral Plane, Ethereal Plane, Void, or space, the astral destroyer glides on magical winds, covering immense distances in short times.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: The astral destroyer senses and can pinpoint the location of magic within 120 feet of it. If it senses magic and can see the creature or object bearing the magic it senses, it sees a faint aura around that creature or object.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The astral destroyer makes one Bite attack, three Claw attacks, and one Tail Whip attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (3d10 + 7) piercing damage, and one magic effect of 5th circle or lower on the target ends.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail Whip
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +13 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d6 + 7) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 20 STR save or be knocked prone or pushed up to 15 feet away from the astral destroyer (the destroyer’s choice).

action.name: Astral Stride
entries: The astral destroyer magically enters the Astral Plane or Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. Other creatures can’t be transported with it.

bonus.name: Consume Reality
entries: The astral destroyer consumes all material in a 10-foot cube within 15 feet of it, instantly destroying all nonmagical material that isn’t being worn or carried and reducing any nonmagical objects in the area that aren’t being worn or carried to 0 HP . Each creature in the area must make a DC 20 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If at least one creature fails the save, the astral destroyer regains 18 (4d8) HP . Otherwise, it regains 10 (3d6) HP . Azer Heat radiates from what a dwarf made of stone with lava-filled cracks. It stands ready with a stone shield in one soot-covered hand while it whirls a massive hammer around its head with the other.

bonus.name: Dedicated Smiths and Sculptors
entries: Azers delight in crafting, whether sculpting volcanic rock into pleasing shapes or hammering a stout shield out of molten metal. Though not all azers are perfectionists, many will spend years making minor tweaks and adjustments until a piece is just right. Azers interrupted while practicing their craft display their fierce and fiery tempers.

bonus.name: Dwarven Allies
entries: The first humanoids that azers ever encountered on the Material Plane were dwarves. Recognizing fellow craftsmen, the ancient dwarves showed the azers great hospitality, and the two peoples exchanged many tools and techniques in that early meeting. Seeing dwarves as their material-bound twins, the elementals reforged their bodies into dwarvish shapes, finding the forms aesthetically pleasing and useful for continued contact with dwarves. Though those ancient dwarves and early azers have long since perished, azers and dwarves have long memories, and many clans from both sides continue to maintain connections and exchange ideas .

bonus.name: Deadly to the Touch
entries: As beings of elemental fire, azers are inimical to those unprotected from flame. They transfer their heat to the weapons they wield, but just standing next to an azer can roast a foe, as heat ripples off them in waves.

trait.name: Heated Body
entries: A creature that touches the azer or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 (1d6) fire damage.

trait.name: Heated Weapons
entries: When the azer hits with a metal melee weapon, the weapon deals an extra 3 (1d6) fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The azer sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The balara makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks, or it makes three Necrotic Bolt attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (3d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage, and the target can’t regain HP until the start of the balara’s next turn.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Necrotic Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (3d8 + 2) necrotic damage, and the target can’t regain HP until the start of the balara’s next turn.

bonus.name: Blazing Bulwark
entries: The azer creates fire in a 15-foot line that is 1 foot wide, originating from a point the azer can see within 15 feet of it. This line is an opaque wall that is 3 feet tall and provides half-cover to creatures behind it. A creature that moves through the line takes damage as if it touched the azer. The line of fire lasts for 1 minute or until the azer uses this bonus action again.

bonus.name: Undead Nature
entries: The balara doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

bonus.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The balara is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

reaction.name: Text
entries: Relentless Pursuit. When a creature the balara can see moves away from it, the balara can move up to half its speed toward that creature. This movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks. Balara An unholy assemblage of animal bones in a large humanoid form lumbers forward. Wisps of a cold, blue light curl outward from the wolf skull atop its shoulders, and strips of rotting skin and fur hang on its bones like a macabre coat.

reaction.name: Druidic Vengeance
entries: When entire herds or flocks are massacred or succumb to a deliberate poisoning or excessive hunting, some druid circles lash out by gathering the corpses and turning them into a balara. The embodiment of druidic vengeance, a balara is a frightening weapon against despoilers of natural fauna.

reaction.name: Frightening Light
entries: A cold, blue light glows at the heart of the balara. Creatures that see the light experience the deaths of the animals that make up the balara, feeling the pain and fear those animals felt just before dying.

reaction.name: Nature Perverted
entries: While most balara are spawned by enraged druids, some balara form spontaneously when the crime against nature is great, such as a magical fog seeping out from a wizard’s laboratory and wiping out all the animals in a nearby forest. Spontaneously formed balara typically hunt down the creatures most responsible before going on a rampage, killing any humanoids they encounter.

monster.name: Balara
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can’t speak

trait.name: Terrifying Luminance
entries: When a creature that can see the balara’s glowing ribcage starts its turn within 30 feet of the balara, the balara can force it to make a DC 14 WIS save if the balara isn’t incapacitated and can see the creature. On a failure, the creature is frightened until the start of its next turn, as the visions, fear, and pain of the last moments of several animals flash through its mind. While frightened, the creature’s speed is reduced to 0, and it can’t use reactions. Unless surprised, a creature can avert its eyes to avoid the save at the start of its turn. If the creature does so, it can’t see the balara until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If the creature looks at the balara in the meantime, it must immediately make the save. Basilisk This gray-green serpent rears up to the height of a pony. It bares teeth strong enough to crush bone, and its eyes glow a lurid yellow.

trait.name: Aggressive Predators
entries: Basilisks eat petrified flesh, their poison dissolving it into a grainy mush, and constantly hunt for prey to petrify. T oo slow to keep up with most prey, basilisks rely on their petrifying gaze and well-timed ambushes. Basilisks are largely indiscriminate hunters, attacking anything that looks even remotely tasty in its territory.

trait.name: Territorial
entries: A basilisk’s aggressive hunting becomes outright hostility toward rival basilisks that enter its territory—but they will not eat the petrified flesh of another basilisk. This leaves many basilisk territories littered with statues of other basilisks who dared intrude. Canny adventurers can take advantage of this by fooling a basilisk into staring at a reflective surface, where it then tries to petrify the perceived competitor.

trait.name: Stone Dwellers
entries: Basilisks haunt areas filled with rocks, such as caves, stony hills, and mountains. Because they eat petrified flesh, they remove traces of their victims, rendering it difficult to notice their lairs.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The basilisk is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Petrifying Gaze
entries: When a creature that can see the basilisk’s eyes starts its turn within 30 feet of the basilisk, the basilisk can force it to make a DC 12 CON save if the basilisk isn’t incapacitated and can see the creature. On a failure, the creature magically begins to turn to stone and is restrained. The restrained creature must repeat the save at the end of its next turn, becoming petrified on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The petrification lasts until the creature is freed by the greater restoration spell or other magic. Unless surprised, a creature can avert its eyes to avoid the save at the start of its turn. If the creature does so, it can’t see the basilisk until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If the creature looks at the basilisk in the meantime, it must immediately make the save. If the basilisk sees itself reflected on a polished surface within 30 feet of it and in an area of bright light, the basilisk mistakes itself for a rival and is affected by its own gaze.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The basilisk makes two Bite or Poison Spit attacks. If both Bite or Poison Spit attacks hit one creature, the target is poisoned until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

action.name: Poison Spit
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) poison damage. Behir Lightning crackles between the fangs of this sinuous reptilian creature. Its leathery blue-black skin shimmers, and tufts of lightning-blue fur stick out in wisps along its spine.

action.name: Underground and Cliff Dwellers
entries: Behirs thrive in underworld caverns and passageways where their sinuous body and ability to climb give them extra maneuverability and an advantage over prey on a cavern floor. When food is scarce, some behirs will venture above ground. However, these appearances are short-lived because their size and rapacious appetite quickly draw unwanted attention.

action.name: Ambusher
entries: Despite its incredible size, a behir’s coloration and its ability to climb difficult surfaces allows it to conceal itself from its victims until it drops on them. They prefer constricting their prey, holding it still to devour it whole.

action.name: Possible Draconic Origins
entries: Because behirs have potent lightning breath, as well as blue hide and reptilian appearance, many scholars have proposed a link between the creatures and blue dragons. Though a clear connection has never been discovered, behirs do exhibit some hoarding tendencies, eagerly collecting brightly colored or shiny objects.

trait.name: Lightning Absorption
entries: Whenever the behir is subjected to lighting damage, it takes no damage and instead regains a number of HP equal to the lightning damage dealt.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The behir is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The behir makes one Bite attack and one Constrict attack. It can replace its Bite attack with a use of Swallow.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) piercing damage plus 27 (6d8) lightning damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one Large or smaller creature. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Large or smaller and the behir isn’t already grappling a creature, the target is grappled (escape DC 16). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the behir can’t Constrict another target.

action.name: Swallow
entries: The behir makes one Bite attack against a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is also swallowed, and the grapple ends. While swallowed, the target is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the behir, and it takes 21 (6d6) acid damage at the start of each of the behir’s turns. A behir can have only one creature swallowed at a time. If the behir takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from the swallowed creature, the behir must succeed on a DC 14 CON save at the end of that turn or regurgitate the creature, which falls prone in a space within 10 feet of the behir. If the behir dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained by it and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting prone.

action.name: Lightning Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The behir exhales lightning in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking 54 (12d8) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Black Pudding The black, amorphous blob glistens in the torchlight as it undulates forward. Predator of the Deep Dark. Like most oozes, the black pudding is a mindless predator that prowls deep caverns, abandoned sewers, dank dungeons, and other dark places underground. Though fire isn’t particularly harmful to the black pudding, it avoids open flames and places filled with light, finding them uncomfortable.

action.name: Glistening Tar
entries: A black pudding has a glistening surface reminiscent of hot tar when exposed to light. The surface of its body reacts to light when exposed for more than a moment, absorbing and reducing the light around it. This natural ability makes the black pudding especially adept at ambushing prey.

action.name: Varied Diet
entries: Capable of dissolving and consuming wood and metal as well as flesh and bone, the black pudding is drawn to the warmth of living creatures, the scent of cut wood, and the unique tang metal leaves in the air. However, the secretions of a black pudding have no effect on stone.

monster.name: Black Pudding
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The pudding can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Corrosive Form
entries: A creature that touches the pudding or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 4 (1d8) acid damage. Any nonmagical weapon made of metal or wood that hits the pudding corrodes. After dealing damage, the weapon takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to −5, the weapon is destroyed. Nonmagical ammunition made of metal or wood that hits the pudding is destroyed after dealing damage. The pudding can eat through a panel of nonmagical wood or metal in its space that is up to 2 inches thick in 1 round.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The pudding doesn’t require sleep.

trait.name: Ooze Resilience
entries: The pudding is resistant to the restrained condition, and it is immune to exhaustion and to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, and prone conditions.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The pudding can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The black pudding makes two Pseudopod attacks.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) acid damage. In addition, nonmagical armor worn by the target is partly dissolved and takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armor reduced to an AC of 10 is destroyed.

bonus.name: Dampen Light
entries: The black pudding dims the light around it. The radius of each light source within 60 feet of it is halved until the start of its next turn. The pudding can’t use this action while in sunlight.

reaction.name: Split
entries: When a pudding that is Medium or larger is subjected to lightning or slashing damage, it splits into two new puddings if it has at least 10 HP . Each new pudding has HP equal to half the original pudding’s, rounded down. New puddings are one size smaller than the original pudding. BLINK DOG FAMILIARS The blink dog’s curiosity and fiercely loyal nature lead some of them to serve spellcasters as familiars, especially if that spellcaster performed a great service to the blink dog’s pack. Blink dogs that serve as familiars have the following trait.

reaction.name: Familiar
entries: The blink dog can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the blink dog senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the blink dog is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the blink dog’s Fey Resilience trait. At any time and for any reason, the blink dog can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond. Blink Dog This canine is lithe and poised, with tawny fur, a long tail, and tall, pointed ears. Intelligence radiates from its deep eyes, which seem to hang in the air a moment as it vanishes, reappearing in the distance.

reaction.name: Teleporting Fey
entries: Blink dogs are intelligent fey creatures with the ability to pop in and out of being, allowing them to teleport short distances. They typically roam in packs in the Plane of the Fey unless in the service of a fey noble. They help lost travelers in their realm, though each pack follows its own codes.

reaction.name: Mysterious Origins
entries: Many stories seek to explain the existence of blink dogs. One popular theory is that a fey lord or lady bred them especially for hunting, their blinking making them adept at surprising prey and guiding it to the hunting party. Others maintain that a pack of dogs mistakenly crossed over into the fey realms and were changed by them.

reaction.name: Highly Intelligent
entries: Blink dogs are as intelligent as people, and while they can’t speak traditional languages, they can understand them. They are also fiercely loyal to the other members of their pack.

reaction.name: Spider Killers
entries: Blink dogs despise phase spiders and will kill them on sight, often using the strength of the pack to do so. Some sages speculate that the phase spiders’ ability to shift between planes creates a dissonance that blink dogs find extremely painful. Others believe the spiders’ phasing causes small tears between planes, and the blink dogs follow an ancient instinct to prevent such tears.

monster.name: Blink Dog
languages: understands Sylvan but can’t speak it

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The blink dog is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The blink dog’s Perception is 18 while perceiving by hearing or smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The blink dog has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the dog’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Teleport (Recharge 4–6)
entries: The blink dog teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 40 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Before or after teleporting, the dog can make one Bite attack. Bulette An armor-plated, squat and muscular beast bursts through the ground while uttering a guttural roar.

action.name: Grasslands Ambushers
entries: Frightful predators of deserts, grasslands, and other flat terrains, bulettes track their prey from below, waiting for the perfect moment to launch a surprise attack.

action.name: Always Moving When Awake
entries: Also referred to as “land sharks,” bulettes have insatiable appetites. They constantly travel just below the surface of the ground when they hunt, using their fine-tuned sensitivity to vibrations to locate food. Aboveground, they leap while running, even if they don’t have any quarry.

action.name: Uncertain Dormancy
entries: After weeks of feeding and ravaging the land, a bulette finds a secluded location and digs deep underground where it can hibernate and digest. The bulette instinctively fills in the tunnel behind it to keep hunters at bay while it rests. The creature is not tied to seasons or any other discernable patterns, leaving locals unable to predict when the bulette will become active again.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The bulette is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The bulette’s long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bulette makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (3d10 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Deadly Leap (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The bulette leaps up to 30 feet, diving onto a point it can see or sense within that range and landing on its feet with a thunderous crash. It occupies a space of its choice within 5 feet of that point, and each creature within 10 feet of that point must make a DC 15 STR save. On a failure, a creature takes 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage and 14 (4d6) thunder damage and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone. Any creature in the bulette’s space is then pushed into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the bulette. If no unoccupied space is within range, the creature instead falls prone in the bulette’s space.

bonus.name: Seek Prey
entries: The bulette looks and sniffs around for new prey, releasing a satisfied, guttural chirp when it settles on one. One creature the bulette can see or sense within 30 feet of it and that can see or hear the bulette must make a DC 15 WIS save. On a failure, the target is frightened until the end of its next turn, and the bulette has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against the creature before the start of its next turn. Centaur A creature with lean human’s upper body affixed to the body of a chestnut horse thunders through the grass. The creature wheels around deftly on its four legs as it nocks an arrow in its longbow.

bonus.name: Plains Runners
entries: Centaurs roam plains, deserts, and other terrain that gives them the freedom to run. Typically nomadic, they stay in one location for a week or less before moving on. The exception is when centaurs have several young, when they take to forests, ravines, or other shelters to raise their children until the children can keep pace with the adults.

bonus.name: Not Draft Animals
entries: Centaurs become deeply offended when asked by outsiders to pull objects or give rides to able-bodied humanoids. However, centaurs helping others escape wildfires or stampedes have been known to scoop up children and offer rides to those less able-bodied. Otherwise, centaurs sleep under the stars and travel light, carrying only what they need upon their backs.

bonus.name: Reluctant Omnivores
entries: Centaurs eat grains, fruit, nuts, and vegetables, though they can supplement their diets with fish and fowl, if necessary.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The centaur is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the centaur moves at least 30 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Pike attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the centaur can make one Hooves attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The centaur makes one Hooves attack and one Pike attack, or it makes three Longbow attacks.

action.name: Hooves
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Pike
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Defensive Kick
entries: When a creature the centaur can see within 5 feet of it hits it with an attack, the centaur can kick the attacker. The attacker must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone or pushed up to 5 feet away from the centaur (the centaur’s choice). Chimera The leonine head of this lion-bodied creature roars. A goat head on its shoulder bleats in response, as smoke spills from the draconic head on its opposite shoulder.

reaction.name: Cursed Origins
entries: A powerful red dragon once coveted a gentle goddess’s treasure, given to a temple by those the goddess had healed. After years of laying waste to the land surrounding its lair, the dragon decided it was powerful enough to defeat the goddess of healing and loot her treasury. The goddess, amused by the mortal creature’s feeble attempts at besting it, left the dragon alive but changed it, shrinking it and merging it with a lion and a goat. The deity then cursed the dragon’s entire bloodline as a warning against excessive pride, and the chimera exists to this day.

reaction.name: Discordant Desires
entries: A chimera has a dim intellect and a basic grasp of the language its ancestor once roared through the skies. Its draconic head still covets coins, gemstones, and other shiny treasures, but its other two heads have different desires. The lion is particularly vain and seeks to always look and feel superior to others, leading the lion to feed its vanity. The goat is delighted by magic and wants to observe, study, and experience it, regardless how dangerous such magic might be.

reaction.name: Monstrous Arguments
entries: The three heads’ differing desires often lead a chimera to argue with itself about travel, lair locations, and schemes. The one thing upon which they all agree is that they enjoy chasing, catching, and devouring prey, though even this begs the question of which head gets the pleasure of devouring their prey.

trait.name: Bickering Heads
entries: The chimera is perpetually one step away from arguing with itself. A creature that can see the chimera can use a bonus action on its turn to appeal to one of the chimera’s heads with bribes (dragon), praise (lion), displays of magic (goat), or other clever manipulation of a head’s desires. The chimera must succeed on a DC 15 WIS save or succumb to that desire. If the lion or goat head succumbs, the chimera has disadvantage on the next Bite or Headbutt attack it makes before the end of its next turn. If the dragon head succumbs, creatures caught in the chimera’s next Fire Breath have advantage on the save, provided the chimera uses the breath within the next 1 minute. Once a head has succumbed to a desire, that head can’t succumb again for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The chimera is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
entries: The chimera has three heads and is resistant to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The chimera makes one Bite attack, one Headbutt attack, and two Claws attacks, or it can make two Claws attacks and use Fire Breath, if available.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Headbutt
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon head exhales fire in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 27 (6d8) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Chol A squad of fiends, their congealed bodies each a single translucent hue, stand ready to engage their enemies with spell and weapon. Their eyes seem to shine with hunger for battle.

action.name: Seasoned Mercenaries
entries: Chol are fiends from the Lower Planes that serve as formidable soldiers to any commander—whether fiend or mortal—willing to pay their price. Their love of inflicting suffering and misery along with their fearlessness in battle make them prized allies—though they expect to be paid handsomely. Unconstrained by either order or chaos, chol always act in their own self-interests, switching sides if an opportunity offers better payment or gratification.

action.name: Planar Travelers
entries: Chol hail from the Lower Planes and are usually found in the fiercest fighting of those regions, unbothered by inhospitable environments. They also freely cross to other worlds and often make their way to the battlefields of mortals, serving the highest bidder wherever war is conducted.

action.name: Varied Forms, Varied Payment
entries: Chol hierarchy is based upon their talents and the payments they accept. Czols are assassins and spies, who demand payment in precious metals, such as gold coins. Uulgols thrive as shock troops, who demand payment in high-quality weapons and armor, such as adamantine chain mail. Icthols are spellcasters specializing in infiltration and impeding enemies, who demand payment in magic items and scrolls. T rillipol lead all other chols, commanding entire chol battalions and orchestrating deals on grand scales, and they demand payment in favors, valuable lore, and often in the heads of powerful leaders.

action.name: Payments Are Power
entries: Chol gain power—and eventually ascend into other types of chol—by absorbing large amounts of their preferred payment. T rillipols don’t ascend and instead absorb the heads of powerful leaders to grow their own powers of leadership, expanding their influence over other chol and the number of chol willing to follow them.

trait.name: Acidic Weapons
entries: The czol’s weapon attacks are magical. When the czol hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 2d8 acid damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If the czol is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a DEX save to take only half damage, the czol instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save, and only half the damage if it fails.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The czol has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The czol makes one Dagger attack and one Wing Slap attack, or it makes three Acid Bolt attacks.

action.name: Dagger
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d4 + 5) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) acid damage.

action.name: Wing Slap
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) acid damage. The target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or take 4 (1d8) acid damage at the start of each of its turns, as acid clings to the wound. Any creature can take an action to purge the acid from the wound with a successful DC 12 WIS (Medicine) check. The acid also leaves the wound if the target receives magical healing, ending the effect.

action.name: Acid Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) acid damage.

bonus.name: Hidden Step
entries: The czol magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see and takes the Hide action.

trait.name: Acidic Weapons
entries: As the czol, except the icthol’s weapon deals an extra 4d8 acid damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: As the czol.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The icthol makes three Claw or Acid Bolt attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 18 (4d8) acid damage.

action.name: Acid Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 27 (5d8 + 5) acid damage. Acid Eruption (Recharge 5–6). The icthol causes a fountain of acid to erupt from a point on the ground it can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a DC 17 DEX save, taking 49 (11d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Then, the ground within 20 feet of that point becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute or until the icthol uses this action again.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The icthol casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using INT as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17).
At will: disguise self, mage hand, minor illusion,
prestidigitation
3/day each: blindness/deafness, dispel magic,
hypnotic pattern
1/day each: confusion, mind blank, seeming

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: The icthol magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

trait.name: Acidic Weapons
entries: As the czol, except the uulgol’s weapon deals an extra 3d8 acid damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: As the czol.

trait.name: Weapon Bond
entries: The uulgol can’t be disarmed of its maul. In addition, if it starts its turn with one or more weapons absorbed, it gains 5 (2d4) temporary HP for each absorbed weapon.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The uulgol makes it makes one Claw attack and one Maul attack, or it makes three Acid Bolt attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) acid damage.

action.name: Maul
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (4d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 13 (3d8) acid damage, and if the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the uulgol or knocked prone (the uulgol’s choice). If the uulgol scores a critical hit, the target has disadvantage on the save and also suffers one level of exhaustion on a failed save.

action.name: Acid Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (4d8 + 2) acid damage.

bonus.name: Absorb Weapon
entries: The uulgol absorbs one weapon it can see within 5 feet of it, other than its maul. If the weapon is being worn or carried by another creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 17 STR save to avoid its weapon being absorbed by the uulgol. An absorbed weapon floats inside the uulgol’s gelatinous body, completely dissolving after 1 hour if it is nonmagical or remaining there until removed if magical. A creature within 5 feet of the uulgol can take its action to retrieve a weapon inside the uulgol by succeeding on a DC 17 STR check. The uulgol can retrieve a weapon inside it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Acidic Weapons
entries: As the czol, except the trillipol’s weapon deals an extra 5d8 acid damage.

trait.name: Bolstering Presence
entries: Each friendly chol, except the trillipol, within 10 feet of the trillipol has advantage on attack rolls and on saves.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: As the czol.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The trillipol makes three Slam attacks, or it makes four Acid Bolt attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 22 (5d8) acid damage.

action.name: Acid Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +10 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 26 (5d8 + 4) acid damage. Acid Burst (Recharge 5–6). The trillipol emits a burst of acid. Each creature within 20 feet of the trillipol must make a DC 19 DEX save, taking 63 (14d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more takes 18 (4d8) acid damage at the start of its next turn as the acid continues to burn it.

bonus.name: Shape Chol
entries: The trillipol magically manipulates the bodies or acid of up to three friendly chol it can see within 60 feet of it, causing one of the following effects.
• Elastic Limbs. Each target’s speed increases by 10 feet,
and the next time it moves before the start of the trillip- ol’s next turn, its movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks.
• Frightful Mien. Each target’s form takes on a more fiend-

ish mien, including sulfurous breath and additional horns or spikes. Each non-chol creature within 5 feet of a target must succeed on a DC 19 WIS save or be frightened until the start of the trillipol’s next turn.
• Sticky Acid. The acid coating each target’s weapons

becomes viscous and sticky. The first time a target hits a creature with a weapon attack before the start of the trillipol’s next turn, the creature hit must succeed on a DC 19 STR save or be restrained in acid until the start of the trillipol’s next turn.

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: As the icthol. Chuul A creature with features blending crab and jellyfish emerges from the water. It has two segmented legs and a pair of oversized pincers, and short tentacles dangle from the shell on its shoulders.

bonus.name: Diminished Circumstances
entries: Primordial chuuls oversaw an aquatic empire rivalling the empires of the aboleths and the merfolk. They learned mighty arcane rituals and used these rituals to control and absorb territory. They overextended their power and suffered ignominious defeats, after which they served the empires they once rivaled. Over the ages, chuuls lost much of their vast intellect and most of their magical might, retaining only the ability to sense the magic they can no longer wield.

bonus.name: Magic Item Scavengers
entries: As the chuuls declined, they became shock troops and hunters of magic items for the underwater empires they served. As those ancient empires fell, chuuls regained their independence and a dim genetic memory of their association with magic. They still search for magic and attack ships and coastal settlements to obtain it. Unable to fully understand how to utilize it, many chuul nonetheless hoard, protect, or even wear magic items they discover.

bonus.name: Carrion Eaters
entries: Chuuls prefer carrion and can be found scavenging old shipwrecks, consuming the leftovers from larger predators, or even leaving their own kills to rot in sunken caves or along coastlines. A beached whale carcass often attracts multiple chuul to the site.

monster.name: Chuul
languages: understands Deep Speech but can’t speak

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The chuul is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The chuul can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: The chuul senses and can pinpoint the location of magic within 120 feet of it. If it senses magic and can see the creature or object bearing the magic it senses, it sees a faint aura around that creature or object.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The chuul makes two Pincer attacks.

action.name: Pincer
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 14). The chuul has two pincers, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Wild Reach (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The chuul taps into ancient memories of magic and reaches toward nearby magic with an unpracticed grasp, releasing a burst of energy. Each creature within 15 feet of the chuul must make a DC 14 DEX save, taking 21 (6d6) damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If a magic item, spell effect, or spellcaster is within 30 feet of the chuul, the burst is pure arcane energy, dealing force damage. If no magic is within 30 feet of the chuul, the burst pulls from the chuul’s own life force, dealing necrotic damage. If the damage is necrotic, the chuul takes necrotic damage as if it succeeded on the save.

bonus.name: Paralyzing Tentacles
entries: One creature grappled by the chuul must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. Until this poison ends, the creature is paralyzed. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Cloaker What at first looked like a simple leather cloak unfolds itself to reveal an enormous bat-like creature with a distended maw and spear-tipped tail. It looses a moan as it lurches forward, ready to cling to its prey.

bonus.name: Cruel Hunters
entries: Cloakers relish luring victims into investigating the cloaks they resemble, hoping to get the drop on their prey. Regardless of where they attach themselves, cloakers wrap around their victims and invite the victims’ allies to attack it, unknowingly damaging their friend with every attack against the cloaker. When a cloaker wants to finish off its prey, it utters a terrifying moan to drive away potential saviors.

bonus.name: Cunning Allies
entries: A cloaker uses its appearance to its advantage, allying with a humanoid or undead creature and acting as a mundane cloak. Sometimes such ruses result in long-term alliances where the wearer and cloaker bond and work together to accomplish their schemes. Most often, though, this alliance ends with the cloaker devouring its unsuspecting ally.

bonus.name: Shadow Manipulator
entries: Cloakers have an innate connection with shadows and darkness and can cover themselves in shadows like a blanket. They use this power to protect themselves when in danger or to ambush prey.

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The cloaker is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Damage Transfer
entries: While attached to a creature, the cloaker takes only half the damage dealt to it (rounded down), and that creature takes the other half.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the cloaker remains motionless without its underside exposed, it is indistinguishable from a dark leather cloak.

trait.name: Light Sensitivity
entries: While in bright light, the cloaker has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 6 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The cloaker makes one Bite attack and two Tail attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) piercing damage, and if the target is Large or smaller, the cloaker attaches to it. If the cloaker has advantage on the attack roll, the cloaker attaches to the target’s head, and the target is blinded and unable to breathe while the cloaker is attached. While attached, the cloaker can’t make Bite attacks, and at the start of each of the cloaker’s turns, the target takes 18 (4d6 + 4) piercing damage. The attached cloaker moves with the target whenever the target moves, requiring none of the cloaker’s movement. It can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement on its turn. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the cloaker by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Moan (Recharge 6)
entries: The cloaker releases a deep, thrumming moan that rattles the nerves of all who hear it. Each creature within 30 feet of the cloaker that can hear the moan and that isn’t an Aberration must make a DC 15 WIS save. On a failure, a creature takes 40 (9d8) psychic damage and is frightened until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t frightened.

bonus.name: Cloak of Shadows (3/Day)
entries: While the cloaker isn’t in bright light, it pulls shadows around itself, partially obscuring its form. For 1 minute or until the cloaker is hit with an attack, the cloaker’s Stealth is 20, and creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against the cloaker. Cockatrice Patchy feathers cover this vulture-sized bird, and a long, reptilian tail extends behind it. Its serrated beak opens, revealing a long, forked tongue, as it shrieks out a hellish rooster’s crow.

bonus.name: Primarily Herbivorous
entries: Cockatrices combine avian and reptilian features, and their beaks can turn victims to stone. However, cockatrices cannot digest stone and use their unique bite to protect themselves from predators. They eat berries and vegetable material, shredding leaves and grass with their serrated beaks, and they consume insects, worms, and other miniscule prey they can easily gulp down.

bonus.name: Cockatrice Fights
entries: Underground arenas led by foolhardy gang lords feature cockatrice combats, often augmenting the creatures with blades as they would with ordinary fowl. Unfortunately, many would-be owners meet their demise in training cockatrices for fights, assuming the creatures are as docile as roosters.

bonus.name: Astonishingly Delicious
entries: Some humanoids discovered long ago that, when prepared correctly, cockatrices taste better than similarly prepared birds. However, raising them has obvious dangers, which leaves them a rare delicacy available only to the most foolhardy or wealthy diners.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The cockatrice is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Serrated Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) slashing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 11 CON save or be restrained as it begins to turn to stone. The creature must repeat the save at the end of its next turn. On a success, the effect ends. On a failure, the creature is petrified for 24 hours.

reaction.name: Shrieking Crow
entries: When a creature the cockatrice can see within 30 feet of it attacks it, the cockatrice can let out a startling crow. The attacker must succeed on a DC 11 WIS save or have disadvantage on the attack roll. Couatl A feathered serpent with multicolored wings glides effortlessly across the sky.

reaction.name: Feathered Serpents
entries: Couatls are feathered serpents that serve as messengers of the gods. Observant and benevolent, they appear as a swirl of rainbow plumage among the clouds or as a rainbow-eyed page delivering an unexpected parcel.

reaction.name: Persistent Rainbows
entries: Couatls often take on bestial or humanoid forms to deliver their messages or observe important events, yet they retain some remnant of their true forms while disguised, such as brightly colored hair, iridescent eyes, or a patch of shimmering skin.

reaction.name: Nonlethal Combatants
entries: Unless they are fighting against unrepentant evil, couatls first attempt to negotiate with potential foes, and merely subdue them should combat prove unavoidable.

reaction.name: Celestial Messengers
entries: Benevolent gods of knowledge and peace send couatls to the Material Plane to observe mortals, deliver messages to followers, and to provide crucial aid to the forces of good and order.

trait.name: Celestial Resilience
entries: The couatl is resistant to radiant damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

trait.name: Inscrutable
entries: The couatl is immune to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, as well as divination spells or effects, such as scrying or detecting its location, that it refuses. WIS (Insight) checks made to ascertain the couatl’s intentions or sincerity have disadvantage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The couatl has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The couatl’s weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The couatl makes one Bite attack and one Constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 8 (1d6 + 5) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) poison damage. If the poison damage reduces the target to 0 HP , the couatl can choose for the target to be stable but poisoned for 1 hour, even after regaining HP , and is unconscious while poisoned in this way.

action.name: Constrict
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 15) if it is a Medium or smaller creature. Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the couatl can’t Constrict another target.

action.name: Healing Hiss (3/Day)
entries: The couatl croons a soothing hiss at a creature it can sense within 5 feet of it. The target magically regains 10 (3d6) HP and is freed from any disease or poison.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The couatl magically transforms into a Large or smaller Beast or Humanoid that has a challenge rating no higher than its own, or back into its true form, which is Celestial. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it or is borne by the new form (the couatl’s choice). It reverts to its true form if it dies. In a new form, the couatl retains its Celestial Resilience and Inscrutable traits and its HP , HD, ability to speak, proficiencies, and INT, WIS, and CHA scores, as well as this bonus action and the Rainbow Shield reaction. Its statistics and capabilities are otherwise replaced by those of the new form, except any class features or legendary actions of that form.

reaction.name: Rainbow Shield
