entries: When a mortal is born, sometimes a linked fey guardian spontaneously forms. Some fey guardians are intentionally called by rituals cast upon a pregnant mortal. Others are summoned when a remarkable child needs to be protected, but the most powerful are created by a dying wish.

bonus.name: Godparent
entries: Fey guardians are imbued with magic that each uses to guard, guide, and protect a humanoid. A guardian is preoccupied with the well-being and fortune of its protégé, but a selfless personality is not guaranteed. Guardians can resent their position or make their charges’ lives miserable in the name of “assistance.” Fey guardians choose how to help their charges, and fey methods don’t always match typical mortal methods or expectations.

bonus.name: Unique Beings
entries: Fey guardians can alter their appearance on whim, depending on their personality and on the needs of their charges. However, each guardian has a preferred appearance; this might be a large beast, an unassuming gnome, or even a glowing figure of pure light.

reaction.name: Reposition
entries: When the feral hunter takes damage, it can move up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

monster.name: Fey Guardian
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The fey guardian is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Guardian
entries: The fey guardian is bound to one Humanoid, which it serves as its ward. If the guardian’s ward dies, the guardian also dies. The guardian instinctively knows the location, physical state, and emotional state of its ward, provided they are on the same plane of existence. If the ward is on a different plane, the guardian knows which plane but not the exact location. In addition, the two can communicate telepathically over any distance, even when not on the same plane.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The fey guardian has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects. Flinderbeast From its perch in a dead tree, the feathered lizard tests the air with its long, forked tongue. It offers a toothy grin before disappearing with a sudden pop.

trait.name: Colorful Lizards
entries: Flinderbeasts are feathered, lizard-like fey the size of small dogs with colorful neck frills. While they are omnivorous, they primarily eat wood—the denser the better. Flinderbeasts create lairs by hollowing out trees or stacking haphazard piles of logs, where they hide and watch for entertainment. They frequently ally with elves and wild forest fey, but dryads find them barely tolerable pests, because they eat the bark off dryad trees.

trait.name: Playful Ambushers
entries: Able to remain still for hours, flinderbeasts are incredibly stealthy, but when they choose to attack, they take advantage of their natural ability to teleport to disorient their foes. A flinderbeast delights in ambushing travelers, fey, and even squirrels and birds, nipping victims then teleporting just out of reach. This chaotic game continues until the fey becomes bored, the victim abandons the game, or the victim proves itself a danger.

trait.name: Wooden Delicacies
entries: Flinderbeasts love to sample rare woods, and gifts of exotic lumber can quickly win them over. Worked wood is like a well-cooked meal to them, and they steal furniture, weapons, shields, and other worked, wooden objects to store in their hollow tree lairs.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The flinderbeast is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The flinderbeast’s Perception 18 when perceiving by hearing or smell.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) acid damage.

action.name: Acid Spit
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) acid damage.

action.name: Breath of Flinders (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The flinderbeast exhales splinters in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 14 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Short Step
entries: The flinderbeast teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 15 feet of it.

reaction.name: Catch Snack
entries: When the flinderbeast is the target of a ranged weapon attack that uses ammunition made mostly of wood, such as an arrow, the flinderbeast can attempt to catch and eat the ammunition. The flinderbeast must make a DC 14 DEX save. On a success, it gains 3 (1d6) temporary HP and prevents the attack’s damage. Fungi A cluster of tall, pallid mushrooms with openings resembling a screaming humanoid mouth sits at the edge of the cavern, tended by a larger purple mushroom with tentacle-like fungal stalks.

reaction.name: Underground Forests
entries: The ever-present moisture and lack of sunlight of the underworld are the perfect conditions for its fungal forests. Much as trees and other plants on the surface gain sentience and mobility, a variety of mushrooms and toadstools think and move of their own accord.

reaction.name: Shrieking Defenders
entries: Shriekers are pale mushrooms that serve as the lookouts and guard dogs of mycolids and other fungal communities. They are nearly immobile and rely on their shrieks to surprise intruders and alert other members of the community. Shriekers can perform this function even when not defending fungal communities, and many creatures enter the underworld to harvest shriekers for use in their own lairs.

reaction.name: Necrotic Feasters
entries: All fungi subsist on decay, but violet fungi especially enjoy feasting on undead creatures. Because of this predilection, violet fungi produce a flesh- rotting blue goop filled with traces of necromantic energies the violets have absorbed from their food.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the shrieker remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary fungus.

trait.name: Plant Resilience
entries: The shrieker is resistant to exhaustion and to the paralyzed, petrified, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Thunderous Burst
entries: The shrieker emits a concentrated burst of sound at one creature it can sense within 30 feet of it. The target must make a DC 10 DEX save, taking 2 (1d4) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Shriek
entries: The shrieker emits a high-pitched shriek audible within 300 feet of it. Each creature within 30 feet of it that isn’t a Plant and that can hear the shriek must succeed on a DC 10 WIS save or be frightened until the shriek ends. The shrieker must use a bonus action on its subsequent turns to continue shrieking. It can stop shrieking at any time. The shriek ends if the shrieker is incapacitated. A frightened creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature that successfully saves is immune to the frightened effect of this shrieker’s Shriek for the next 24 hours.

reaction.name: Startled Shriek
entries: When bright light or a creature other than a Plant moves to within 30 feet of the shrieker, the shrieker can use Shriek, if it isn’t already shrieking.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the violet fungus remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary fungus.

trait.name: Plant Resilience
entries: The violet fungus is resistant to exhaustion and to the paralyzed, petrified, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Rotting Touch
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 5 (2d4) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Text
entries: Necrotic Spores (8 HP

bonus.name: or Fewer)
entries: The violet fungus sends out a burst of necrotic-laced spores, rapidly rotting nearby flesh. Each creature within 10 feet of the violet fungus that isn’t a Construct must make a DC 10 CON save, taking 5 (2d4) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Gargoyle Clinging to its perch with stony claws, this winged statue with a fearsome mien unblinkingly watches all who pass beneath it.

bonus.name: Minor Elementals
entries: The first gargoyles were molded by spellcasters on the Material Plane using local stone and stone from the Plane of Earth. They were barely more than animated stone, carved to look menacing and tasked with protecting locations and structures of great importance to humanoid societies. Eventually, they outlived their creators, and over time, they developed their own personalities and communities, carving new gargoyles from portions of their own bodies mixed with stone from the Material Plane.

bonus.name: Stony Defenders
entries: Though their original creators and those early gargoyles are long gone, gargoyles still have a drive to defend their homes, often violently. Some families of gargoyles have affinities for specific types of humanoid architecture and claim the highest reaches of such locations as their homes. Gargoyles need little in the way of sustenance outside of an occasional meal of stone, and they spend most days resting motionless in the warmth of the sun. This leads most humanoids to view them as a mild inconvenience at worst, with some humanoids viewing their presence as a blessing—provided the gargoyles have a liaison among the humanoids to ensure the gargoyles don’t see locals as intruders on the structure. Some humanoid settlements even go so far as to adjust the architecture of their structures, hoping to attract specific gargoyle families as defenders.

trait.name: Diving Pounce
entries: If the gargoyle is flying and moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claws attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the gargoyle can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the gargoyle remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an inanimate statue.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gargoyle makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage.

reaction.name: Fearsome Mien
entries: When a creature the gargoyle can see hits it with an attack, the gargoyle can shift its face to appear more monstrous. The attacker must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be frightened of the gargoyle until the end of its next turn. Gelatinous Cube A floating skeleton surrounded by a wreath of coins floats forward, suspended within a quivering, transparent cube of goo.

reaction.name: Dungeon Cleaners
entries: Created to clean the alchemical spills, living waste, and failed experiments found in the depths of wizardly dungeons, gelatinous cubes are oozes capable of digesting softer living or once-living material, such as cloth, flesh, leather, and even wood, given enough time. On occasion, a gelatinous cube escapes its dungeon home and roams freely in the local sewer system, an ancient cavern, or even an abandoned mine.

reaction.name: Treasure Trail
entries: Gelatinous cubes can’t digest harder materials, such as bone and metal, and these materials are eventually passed out of the cube as it moves. A trail of undigested coins, immaculately clean metal armor, and gleaming swords in an otherwise empty dungeon is a sure sign of a gelatinous cube.

reaction.name: Invisible Danger
entries: Gelatinous cubes are transparent, except when digesting a meal or when some of that meal’s indigestible belongings remain within it, and are big enough to fill a standard hallway, closet, or small room. Many hapless adventurers exploring abandoned wizard towers have carelessly stepped into empty hallways or dark corners of rooms only to find themselves suddenly suspended inside one of these oozes.

monster.name: Gelatinous Cube
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Ooze Cube
entries: The cube takes up its entire space. Other creatures can enter the space, but are subjected to the cube’s Engulf and have disadvantage on the save. Creatures inside the cube can be seen but have total cover. A creature within 5 feet of the cube can take an action to pull a creature or object out of the cube. Doing so requires a successful DC 12 STR check, and the creature making the attempt takes 10 (3d6) acid damage. The cube can hold only one Large creature or up to four Medium or smaller creatures inside it at a time.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The cube doesn’t require sleep.

trait.name: Ooze Resilience
entries: The cube is resistant to the grappled and restrained conditions, and it is immune to exhaustion and to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, and prone conditions.

trait.name: Transparent
entries: While motionless, the cube’s Stealth is 15, even when the cube is in plain sight.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 10 (3d6) acid damage.

action.name: Engulf
entries: The cube moves up to its speed. While doing so, it can enter a Large or smaller creature’s space. When the cube enters a creature’s space, the creature must make a DC 13 DEX save. On a success, the creature can choose to be pushed 5 feet back or to the side of the cube. A creature that chooses not to be pushed suffers the consequences of a failed save. On a failed save, the cube enters the creature’s space, the creature takes 10 (3d6) acid damage, and is engulfed. The engulfed creature can’t breathe, is restrained, and takes 21 (6d6) acid damage at the start of each of the cube’s turns. When the cube moves, the engulfed creature moves with it. An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a DC 13 STR check. On a success, the creature escapes and enters a space of its choice within 5 feet of the cube.

reaction.name: Skewer Prey
entries: When the gelatinous cube is subjected to piercing damage, it can move a random creature engulfed by it to intercept the attack. The creature takes the piercing damage as if it were the target. Genie, Djinni This graceful humanoid stands as tall as two humans, and it dances through the air on a column of clouds.

reaction.name: Gracious Hosts
entries: Djinn are genies originating from realms of elemental air. They are fun-loving and believe that welcoming strangers is far more productive than opening with hostilities. They extend hospitality and the benefit of the doubt even to guests who arrive to their homes with weapons drawn, knowing words can be just as potent. If their open arms are met with violence regardless, djinn typically retreat from the situation, preferring to resolve issues peacefully and with a bottle or two of wine whenever possible.

reaction.name: Deadly Air Manipulators
entries: Should djinn find themselves regretfully in combat, they deftly maneuver around the battlefield. Their homes have lofty ceilings that give them freedom to move, and each room in their home has at least one window or door to move easily between the building and its exterior. While at range, they unleash whirlwinds to confine and reposition foes. When they enter melee, they fight gleefully and banter with their opponents, expressing genuine admiration at any returned witty quips.

trait.name: Nebulous (Cloud Form Only)
entries: The djinni’s Stealth is 21, it can hover while flying, can’t manipulate objects or attack, and it can pass through any space air can pass through without squeezing. In addition, it can enter and hide in any Tiny or larger container, such as a pouch, lamp, backpack, or barrel.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The djinni makes three Scimitar or Storm Bolt attacks.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) lightning damage or thunder damage (the djinni’s choice).

action.name: Storm Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) lightning damage plus 7 (2d6) thunder damage.

action.name: Create Air Elemental (1/Day)
entries: The djinni magically creates one air-based Elemental of CR 5 or lower in an unoccupied space the djinni can see within 30 feet of it. The elemental acts as an ally of the djinni, obeying its spoken commands. The elemental remains for 1 hour, until the djinni dies, or until the djinni dismisses it as a bonus action.

action.name: Travel the Planes (1/Day)
entries: The djinni transports itself to a different plane of existence. This works like the plane shift spell, except it can affect only itself and can’t use this action to banish an unwilling creature.

bonus.name: Create Whirlwind
entries: The djinni creates whirlwinds of air around up to two creatures it can see within 60 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 STR check or be restrained by a whirlwind. A creature, including the target, can take its action to free the restrained target by succeeding on a DC 17 STR check. While at least one creature is restrained by one of these whirlwinds, the djinni can use this bonus action to move the whirlwind up to 30 feet, and the restrained target moves with it.

bonus.name: Genie Shape
entries: The djinni magically transforms into a Medium cloud of sparkling, blue air or back into its bipedal form. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its bipedal form if it dies. The djinni can’t use this bonus action to transform into its bipedal form while inside a Medium or smaller container. Genie, Efreeti Flames flicker across the horns and down the arm of this grinning, red humanoid as it reaches forward and unleashes a blast of fire.

bonus.name: Dueling Genies
entries: Efreet are genies from realms of elemental fire. They take great pleasure in honing their martial skills and are quick to challenge guests to duels to first blood. They enjoy challenging the same opponents regularly to see how each other’s skills have developed. They don’t kill their opponents unless necessary, as killing an opponent means never getting to test their skills against an improved opponent later. Regardless of the outcome of such duels, efreet are quick to celebrate a good fight with food, wine, and song.

bonus.name: Heat-Resistant Jewelry
entries: Efreet radiate heat, but they aren’t constantly on fire. However, most fire they create runs down and along their arms, and they typically keep their torsos and arms lightly covered, preferring heat-resistant jewelry for adornments. They favor brass for its shine and platinum for its durability, and efreet tend to respond favorably when these metals are involved in any bargains .

trait.name: Nebulous (Cloud Form Only)
entries: The efreeti’s Stealth is 16, it can hover while flying, can’t manipulate objects or attack, and it can pass through any space air can pass through without squeezing. In addition, it can enter and hide in any Tiny or larger container, such as a pouch, lamp, backpack, or barrel.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The efreeti makes three Scimitar or Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) fire damage.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (6d6 + 3) fire damage.

action.name: Create Fire Elemental (1/Day)
entries: The efreeti magically creates one fire-based Elemental of CR 5 or lower in an unoccupied space the efreeti can see within 30 feet of it. The elemental acts as an ally of the efreeti, obeying its spoken commands. The elemental remains for 1 hour, until the efreeti dies, or until the efreeti dismisses it as a bonus action.

action.name: Travel the Planes (1/Day)
entries: The efreeti transports itself to a different plane of existence. This works like the plane shift spell, except it can affect only itself and can’t use this action to banish an unwilling creature.

bonus.name: Blazing Palisade
entries: The efreeti magically creates a translucent wall of fire along a solid surface at a point it can see within 30 feet of it. The wall can be up to 1 foot thick, up to 45 feet long, and up to 5 feet high, and it can be any shape. Each creature in a space where the fire appears must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or take 7 (2d6) fire damage. A creature that successfully saves can move to the wall’s edge, out of the fire. A creature that enters the wall for the first time on a turn or ends its turn within 5 feet of the wall must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or take 7 (2d6) fire damage. The efreeti can have only one fire wall active at a time. If it creates another, the previous fire wall disappears.

bonus.name: Genie Shape
entries: The efreeti magically transforms into a Medium cloud of thick smoke filled with sparks of fire or back into its bipedal form. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its bipedal form if it dies. The efreeti can’t use this bonus action to transform into its bipedal form while inside a Medium or smaller container. Genie, Hinn A stout and rugged humanoid figure emerges from the earth as if from water, dressed in fine clothes. Its amber eyes gleam from within its earthen face.

bonus.name: Steadfast and Social
entries: Hinn are genies hailing from realms of elemental earth. Unlike their brethren, they are the most steadfast and least capricious of the genies. Hinn beliefs are not impossible to change, but it takes much time and effort. Though hinn can take gaseous form like other genies, they prefer to stay solid. That connection to the physical world allows them to relate more closely to mortal creatures, and of all genies, they are the most likely to befriend mortals. Still, most hinn consider themselves superior to mortals and react with hostility to any creature that tries to take advantage of their friendliness.

bonus.name: Immense Wealth
entries: As earth creatures, hinn have access to wealth in the form of precious metals and gemstones. Because of the ease with which they can obtain such materials, only the rarest of these truly arouses their interest. Instead, many hinn fixate on unique objects and collect those as valuables, with each hinn preferring different types of objects. One hinn may collect dolls from across the planes, while another might seek out theatrical plays, observing each play then collecting its script.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The hinn can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the hinn doesn’t disturb the material through which it moves.

trait.name: Nebulous (Cloud Form Only)
entries: The hinn’s Stealth is 20, it can hover while flying, can’t manipulate objects or attack, and it can pass through any space air can pass through without squeezing. In addition, it can enter and hide in any Tiny or larger container, such as a pouch, lamp, backpack, or barrel.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hinn makes three Gem-Studded Mace or Lob Stone attacks. If two Gem-Studded Mace attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Gem-Studded Mace
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) piercing damage.

action.name: Lob Stone
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Create Earth Elemental (1/Day)
entries: The hinn magically creates one earth-based Elemental of CR 5 or lower in an unoccupied space the hinn can see within 30 feet of it. The elemental acts as an ally of the hinn, obeying its spoken commands. The elemental remains for 1 hour, until the hinn dies, or until the hinn dismisses it as a bonus action.

action.name: Travel the Planes (1/Day)
entries: The hinn transports itself to a different plane of existence. This works like the plane shift spell, except it can affect only itself and can’t use this action to banish an unwilling creature.

bonus.name: Churned Earth
entries: The hinn causes the ground on a point it can see within 60 feet of it to erupt. Each creature within 15 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 16 DEX save or take 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone.

bonus.name: Genie Shape
entries: The hinn magically transforms into a Medium cloud of dust filled with miniscule clashing, bursting, and reforming rocks or back into its bipedal form. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its bipedal form if it dies. The hinn can’t use this bonus action to transform into its bipedal form while inside a Medium or smaller container. Genie, Sila A lithe and sinuous humanoid emerges from a column of water, its eyes a deep blue with a depth akin to the ocean. It holds two wave-shaped daggers at the ready.

bonus.name: Fluid and Mercurial
entries: Sila are genies from realms of elemental water. They have no fixed gender or appearance, but they often take on the forms of humanoids when dealing with those creatures. They often view themselves as superior to creatures who have solid, fixed forms. Capricious and mischievous, some sila are haughty and cruel toward solid creatures, while others are playful pranksters, and a few take pity on the creatures, which they see as trapped in fixed forms.

bonus.name: Dagger Wielders
entries: Sila typically carry two jambiyas, daggers with wave-shaped blades that they can wield underwater as well as in air. The jambiyas are considered traditional weapons of these genies and are typically the only solid objects a sila values.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The sila can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Fluid Body (Bipedal Form Only)
entries: The sila can move through the space of any creature, regardless of size, and a creature of any size can move through a sila’s space. In addition, the sila can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Nebulous (Cloud Form Only)
entries: The sila’s Stealth is 21, it can hover while flying, can’t manipulate objects or attack, and it can pass through any space air or water can pass through without squeezing. In addition, it can enter and hide in any Tiny or larger container, such as a pouch, lamp, backpack, or barrel.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sila makes four Cold-Infused Jambiya or Water Burst attacks. If two Water Burst attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the sila.

action.name: Cold-Infused Jambiya
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) slashing damage plus 5 (2d4) cold damage.

action.name: Water Burst
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (4d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Create Water Elemental (1/Day)
entries: The sila magically creates one water-based Elemental of CR 5 or lower in an unoccupied space the sila can see within 30 feet of it. The elemental acts as an ally of the sila, obeying its spoken commands. The elemental remains for 1 hour, until the sila dies, or until the sila dismisses it as a bonus action.

action.name: Travel the Planes (1/Day)
entries: The sila transports itself to a different plane of existence. This works like the plane shift spell, except it can affect only itself and can’t use this action to banish an unwilling creature.

bonus.name: Clouded Eyes
entries: The sila condenses the air or water around the head or eyes of up to two creatures it can see within 30 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or be blinded until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Genie Shape
entries: The sila magically transforms into a Medium cloud of thick mist that leaves droplets of water on everything it touches or back into its bipedal form. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its bipedal form if it dies. The sila can’t use this bonus action to transform into its bipedal form while inside a Medium or smaller container. Ghost A translucent humanoid dressed in a style popular ages ago flies through obstacles as it reaches out its arms.

bonus.name: Restless Spirits
entries: Ghosts are the remains of a creature that died without fulfilling an overwhelming desire, such as unfinished tasks, wrongs they committed and wanted to set right, revenge against their enemies, or even identification of their murderers. Ghosts are driven to fulfill these desires and must do so before they can move on to the true afterlife.

bonus.name: Wide-Ranging Attitudes
entries: Most ghosts become angry with being stuck in the mortal world, their unfinished business hanging on them with palpable weight. They lash out at all who come near, harming their chances of receiving help moving on. However, some ghosts have accepted their fate, or even embrace it, seeing it as an opportunity to observe and aid those who might make their same mistakes.

monster.name: Ghost
languages: any languages it knew in life

trait.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: The ghost can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane, and vice versa.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The ghost can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The ghost doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The ghost is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Unfinished Business
entries: Unless its unfinished business is fulfilled, a destroyed ghost returns in 1d4 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again within 10 feet of where it died. If presented with an object or piece of information related to its unfinished business, the ghost must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn. After it has been incapacitated by a particular source, the ghost can’t be incapacitated by that same source again for 24 hours. If presented with everything it needs to complete its unfinished business, the ghost permanently dies after completing it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ghost makes two Withering Touch or Telekinetic Throw attacks.

action.name: Withering Touch
entries: Melee Spell Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) necrotic damage.

action.name: Telekinetic Throw
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (6d4 + 2) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, depending on the type of object thrown. The ghost can throw only Small or smaller objects within range that aren’t being worn or carried.

action.name: Horrify (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The ghost speaks a haunting phrase, reveals the wounds from its death, or performs some other expression to scare creatures in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 14 WIS save. On a failure, a creature takes 21 (6d6) psychic damage and is frightened until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t frightened. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more increases its age in years by 10 percent, rounded up. This aging can be reversed with a greater restoration spell or similar magic, but only within 24 hours of the aging occurring.

bonus.name: Etherealness
entries: The ghost enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. It is visible on the Material Plane while it is in the Ethereal Plane, and vice versa, yet it can’t affect or be affected by anything on the other plane. Ghoul This gaunt, gray humanoid wears rags and smells strongly of rotting flesh. It flicks a long tongue out of its mouth, sensing for its next warm meal.

bonus.name: Hungry Undead
entries: Ghouls are undead humanoids with a hunger for humanoid flesh and often inhabit cemeteries and crypts to feast. They carry none of their concerns— and little to no memories—from their former lives. They can spread their hunger to others, and humanoids that succumb to this disease transform into ghouls. Fortunately for many communities, ghouls tend to devour their victims, leaving nothing behind to transform.

bonus.name: Varying Origins
entries: Ghouls can be found anywhere humanoids dwell, leading to differing stories of their origins. Some believe ghouls are a curse on humanoids for hubris committed by ancient people, others believe they were created by a demon lord of undeath to amass an unstoppable army, and a few believe they are a blessing from a god of death whose agents consume only the flesh of those worthy of the afterlife.

bonus.name: Pack Leaders
entries: On occasion, a powerful humanoid succumbs to the ghouls’ disease and transforms into a ghast instead. Ghasts are more cunning than their ghoul counterparts, and most ghouls instinctively follow them. Ghasts often retain several memories of their former lives and use these in their hunts, luring in former comrades as meals or new packmates.

bonus.name: Necrophage
entries: Rarely, unique ghasts rise from humanoid spellcasters. These ghasts are known as necrophage ghasts and have a thirst for arcane knowledge almost as strong as their hunger for flesh. Their bodies are infused with magic, altering their natural stench and their bites. However, this infusion makes necrophage ghasts unable to spread the ghoul’s disease, inadvertently making less scrupulous humanoid spellcasters more willing to share knowledge and work with them.

trait.name: Hungry Dead Nature
entries: The ghoul doesn’t require air or sleep. In addition, it must consume at least 2 pounds of raw meat every 24 hours, or it loses its immunity to exhaustion and risks starvation until it does so. While it has any levels of exhaustion from starvation, the ghoul can’t remove levels of exhaustion until it consumes at least 4 pounds of raw meat.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ghoul can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The ghoul is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ghoul makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack. If both attacks hit a creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead, the target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or contract ghoul hunger (see the Ghoul Hunger sidebar).

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage. If the target is a creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Hungry Dead Nature
entries: The ghast doesn’t require air or sleep. In addition, it must consume at least 2 pounds of raw meat every 24 hours, or it loses its immunity to exhaustion and risks starvation until it does so. While it has any levels of exhaustion from starvation, the ghast can’t remove levels of exhaustion until it consumes at least 4 pounds of raw meat.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ghast can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Stench
entries: A creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of the ghast must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. On a success, the creature is immune to the ghast’s Stench for 24 hours.

trait.name: Turning Defiance
entries: The ghast and any ghouls within 30 feet of it have advantage on saves against effects that turn undead.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The ghast is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition. GHOUL HUNGER A disease spread by ghouls, ghoul hunger instills a hunger for Humanoid flesh in the creature suffering it, fully manifesting after 1d4 hours. Once the disease is fully manifested, the infected creature is poisoned until the disease is cured. This effect can be suppressed for 1 hour if the infected creature consumes any amount of Humanoid flesh. Every 12 hours that elapse, the infected creature must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or its HP maximum is reduced by 7 (2d6). The creature has disadvantage on this save if it consumed any amount of Humanoid flesh while diseased. This reduction lasts until the creature finishes

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ghast makes one Bite attack and two Claws attacks. If one Bite attack and at least one Claws attack hit a creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead, the target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or contract ghoul hunger (see the Ghoul Hunger sidebar). a long rest after the disease is cured. The creature dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0. A creature that succeeds on two of these saves recovers from the disease. If an infected Humanoid fails two of these saves or dies while infected, it becomes a ghoul, or a ghast if its PB is +4 or higher, in a flesh-rending transformation. If the humanoid is a spellcaster with a PB +4 or higher, it has a 25 percent chance of transforming into a necrophage ghast instead. Only a wish spell or similarly powerful magic can reverse the transformation.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage. If the target is a creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Arcane Infusion
entries: While the ghast is conscious and wearing no armor and wielding no shield, it adds its INT modifier to its AC (included above).

trait.name: Hungry Dead Nature
entries: The ghast doesn’t require air or sleep. In addition, it must consume at least 2 pounds of raw meat every 24 hours, or it loses its immunity to exhaustion and risks starvation until it does so. While it has any levels of exhaustion from starvation, the ghast can’t remove levels of exhaustion until it consumes at least 4 pounds of raw meat.

trait.name: Necrophage Stench
entries: A creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of the ghast must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or have disadvantage on all saves against spells cast by the ghast for 1 minute. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the ghast’s Necrophage Stench for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ghast can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Turning Defiance
entries: The ghast and any ghouls within 30 feet of it have advantage on saves against effects that turn undead.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The ghast is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ghast makes one Bite attack and two Claws attacks, or it makes three Arcane Blast attacks. It can replace one Bite attack or two Arcane Blast attacks with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) force damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) slashing damage. If the target is a creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Arcane Blast
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) force damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The ghast casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using INT as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14).
At will: guidance, mage hand, prestidigitation
3/day each: command, charm, hold, ray of enfeeblement
1/day each: fear, invisibility (self only)
Giant, Cloud This towering giant has skin the blue-gray of a stormy sky and hair floating around its head like clouds bathed in a sunrise. It gazes at the world beneath it with fierce eyes.

action.name: High Above All
entries: Cloud giants are above other creatures, figuratively and literally, lairing in mighty castles on mountain peaks or on floating cloud islands drifting over the land. They consider themselves superior to most creatures, including other giants, except perhaps storm giants. Some lean into this sense of superiority and use their innate might to subjugate others, ruling all land visible from the tops of their mountain homes. Others keep themselves wholly apart from those bound to trod the land, seeing earthly concerns as beneath their notice and turning their gaze to the heavens and the concerns of flying creatures.

action.name: Cloud Shapers
entries: Cloud giants have an innate connection to clouds and can walk across clouds as earthly creatures walk across the land. They can gather and form clouds into a variety of shapes, often creating whole cities from cloud stuff. Earthly visitors to cloud giant cities must be careful, though. Not all such places are protected by magic that would allow an earthly visitor to walk the clouds as easily as the giants.

trait.name: Cloudsight
entries: The cloud giant can see through areas obscured by clouds, fog, smoke, and steam without penalty.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The cloud giant is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Heightened Smell
entries: The cloud giant’s Perception is 22 when perceiving by smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The cloud giant makes three Cloud-Coated Mace attacks, or it makes two Wind Burst attacks.

action.name: Cloud-Coated Mace
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d6 + 8) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) cold damage.

action.name: Wind Burst
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +8 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 31 (5d10 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the cloud giant and knocked prone.

bonus.name: Cloud Cover
entries: The cloud giant creates a 20-foot cube of clouds centered on a point it can see within 60 feet of it. The cube spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. The cloud lasts for 1 minute, until the cloud giant dies, or until the cloud giant uses this bonus action again.

bonus.name: Condense
entries: The cloud giant causes a 20-foot-radius sphere of cloud, fog, smoke, or steam centered on a point it can see within 60 feet of it to become as thick as mud, making the area difficult terrain for creatures moving through it. A creature that starts its turn in the hardened cloud must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be restrained until the start of its next turn. The cloud remains condensed for 1 minute, until the cloud giant dies, or until the cloud giant uses this bonus action again.

reaction.name: Cirrus Deflection
entries: The cloud giant creates a bit of hardened cloud between it and an attacker, increasing the giant’s AC by 4. To do so, it must be able to see the attacker. Giant, Dirgesinger A giant, clad in ragged black garments, sings a song so deep it rumbles bones and scatters pebbles. It carries an immense, sickle- shaped sword in one hand, and winding symbols decorate its skin.

reaction.name: Singing Giants
entries: Less numerous than other giants, dirgesingers are the descendants of giants who worshipped an ancient god of death. They are known for the deep, rhythmic dirges they sing, and their songs echo for miles in the deep caverns and mountains they call home.

reaction.name: Death-Dealing Saviors
entries: With their connection to the god of their ancestors, dirgesingers can bring eternal rest to undead by singing a special dirge over the creature’s remains. Most dirgesinger clans view this as a sacred duty, but others believe slaying both the living and the dead sates their ancient god, preventing it from waking and bringing an end to all life.

reaction.name: Tattooed at Birth
entries: The holy scripture of the dirgesingers is only sung. Its full recital takes days, and the song is passed down from clan elders. When a dirgesinger is born, a passage from the work is tattooed on its skin. Dirgesingers add their victories to the song inscribed on their bodies, giving each clan its own secrets, sagas, and sacred oaths.

trait.name: Divine Weapons
entries: The dirgesinger’s weapon attacks are magical. When the dirgesinger hits with a weapon attack, the weapon deals an extra 4d8 necrotic damage or radiant damage (included in the attack), the dirgesinger’s choice.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The dirgesinger is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Restful Dirge
entries: A creature slain by the dirgesinger can’t become Undead. In addition, a dirgesinger can spend 10 minutes singing a magical dirge over the remains of an Undead it has destroyed. If it does, that Undead can’t be restored or gain a new body, such as through the Rejuvenation or Unfinished Business traits. If the Undead has a phylactery, mummified heart, or similar object that allows such rejuvenation, the dirgesinger must perform this dirge over that object rather than the creature’s remains.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dirgesinger giant makes two Sickle Sword attacks.

action.name: Sickle Sword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) slashing damage plus 18 (4d8) necrotic damage or radiant damage (the giant's choice).

bonus.name: Bellow
entries: The dirgesinger bellows one of the following songs at up to two creatures it can see within 60 feet of it.
• Debilitating Song. The dirgesinger sings a deep,
rumbling song that disrupts the bodies of creatures. Each target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be poisoned until the end of its next turn.
• Dissonant Hymn. The dirgesinger sings a discordant

song that rattles creatures to the core. Each target must succeed on a DC 16 INT save or be frightened until the end of its next turn. While frightened, the creature must move away from the giant by the safest available route.
• Mournful Dirge. The dirgesinger sings a dirge full of

sorrow, regrets, and missed opportunities. Each target must succeed on a DC 16 WIS save or have disadvantage on saves until the end of its next turn. Giant, Fire The bearded giant with a body like cooling lava hefts a flaming sword over its shoulder and steps forward with a clink of its heavy, metal armor.

bonus.name: Volcanic Giants
entries: Fire giants dwell in areas with volcanic activity, carving out fortresses in the sides of calderas or building them atop geothermal plains in the highlands. Left without heat for long periods of time, a fire giant’s skin begins to itch and crack with even the slightest movement. Because of this, they regularly bathe in lava and, when no lava is available, in hot springs. They abhor the cold and keep fires burning almost constantly when dwelling in colder climes. This occasionally puts them at odds with frost giants, who see the fire giants’ melting of the ice and snow around their homes as a disruption of the natural order of the arctic.

bonus.name: Talented Smiths
entries: Fire giants enjoy metallurgy and use the heat of their homes to heat, purify, and forge metals into various tools, armor, and weapons. Many fire giant smiths mold metal by hand while it is submerged in lava, taking pride in the personal touch it affords them over traditional smithing tools. The giants trade these goods with peoples nearby, most often dwarves, and take great offense if the quality of the metal is ever called into question. Several ancient wars between some dwarf and fire giant clans were started because one smith openly challenged the skill of another.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The fire giant is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The fire giant makes two Flaming Greatsword or Lava Boulder attacks.

action.name: Flaming Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (6d6 + 7) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) fire damage.

action.name: Lava Boulder
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d10 + 3) bludgeoning damage plus 13 (3d8) fire damage.

action.name: Volcanic Burst (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The fire giant expels built-up heat from within itself, sending a blast of lava outward. Each creature within 15 feet of the fire giant must make a DC 16 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 36 (8d8) fire damage and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone. A creature that fails this save has disadvantage on the next save it makes against the giant’s Pyroclastic Surge before the start of the giant’s next turn.

bonus.name: Wintry Blast
entries: A blast of wintry air extends in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide from the frost giant’s outstretched hand. Each creature in the line must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or its speed is halved until the end of its next turn. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is petrified in ice for 1 hour or until a creature spends 1 minute warming the petrified creature. Giant, Frost The hides of polar bears hang across the body of this blue, icy giant with a snowy beard. The giant raises an axe made of ice, which glistens as sunlight flows through it.

bonus.name: Northern Nomads
entries: Frost giants live in small, nomadic family groups in arctic climes, traveling along glaciers and living off hunted game. Some groups raid humanoid villages in their annual travels, taking livestock and tools during years of lighter game, while other groups barter with humanoid villages, trading for metal tools with furs and animal parts of creatures inaccessible to the more cold-sensitive humanoids.

bonus.name: Ice Sculptors
entries: Frost giants find great pleasure in ice, snow, and the frozen world, enjoying how light glistens through the icicles hanging from a tree or the quiet beauty of freshly fallen snow. They sculpt ice and snow into useful tools, such as the axes they favor, but they also sculpt for pleasure, creating ice sculptures of animals they consume, deities they worship, or heroes of that year’s trek through the arctic. The temporary nature of the sculptures and the constant movement of the family excites the giants into creating new sculptures everywhere they go, expressing each year’s triumphs and troubles through ice.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The frost giant is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Ice Walk
entries: The frost giant can move across icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. In addition, difficult terrain composed of ice or snow doesn’t cost it extra movement.

trait.name: Snowsight
entries: The frost giant can see through areas obscured by snowfall, sleet, and other wintry precipitation without penalty.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant makes two Icy Greataxe or Ice Boulder attacks. Giant, Hill This slouching, brutish giant’s hairy form is clad in crude hides. It wields a massive tree limb as a club, its broken branches poking out like spikes, and carries a satchel dripping with gore and buzzing with flies.

action.name: Giants of the Land
entries: More earthly than their cloud giant cousins and less sophisticated craftsmen than their fire giant cousins, hill giants dwell in palisaded settlements in the forests and valleys in the foothills of mountains. Their homes are typically made of loosely cut logs or uprooted trees with some homes still sporting branches or even bits of roots on the exterior. They primarily live off the land, but they often take what they need from creatures smaller and weaker than themselves, seeing this as the natural order of the world.

action.name: Mighty Leaders
entries: Might makes right among these giants, and the leaders of their settlements are the toughest—and often largest—giants in the village. Hill giants are unerringly loyal and obedient to leaders they feel are stronger than them, and they can be fierce allies to other creatures that have the right leader at the helm.

action.name: Gluttons
entries: Hill giants are a bit gluttonous, gorging on meals whenever the season is bountiful. They always carry gnawed-on carcasses, barrels of pilfered foods, and uprooted berry bushes in pouches, pockets, and satchels as ready snacks. Easily distracted, they rarely consume all such saved food, leaving the smell of rot ever- present around these containers.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The hill giant is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Show of Might
entries: The hill giant can use STR instead of CHA when making CHA (Intimidation) checks.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant makes two Spiked Tree Branch or Rotten Snack attacks.

action.name: Spiked Tree Branch
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 5 (2d4) piercing damage.

action.name: Rotten Snack
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be poisoned until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Body Bounce
entries: The hill giant bumps its stomach, hip, shoulder, knee, or other part of its body into a creature within 10 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the giant and knocked prone. Giant, Stone This lean, muscular, and hairless giant appears chiseled from granite. A large bag is slung over its shoulder, and it palms a small boulder in one hand.

bonus.name: Giants of the Deep Earth
entries: Stone giants live simple, ascetic lives, lairing with extended families in caves among the rocky highlands. They herd animals for food, leather, and fur, though they spend the majority of their time underground. Each extended family prefers a particular type of stone or crystal and use it in their decorations, both on themselves and in their homes. These stones are believed to have specific properties that exemplify or enhance some trait the family values, such as courage, optimism, wisdom, determination, compassion, or similar.

bonus.name: Lovers of Games
entries: While generally stoic, stone giants love friendly competition and physical sports, such as rock-throwing and wrestling. The echo of cracks and booms in the mountains often signals one of these competitions, which the giants are careful to perform only in areas and seasons where avalanches aren’t a concern. Many stone giant courtships and inter-family rivalries happen at these games, strengthening the bonds between the families.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The stone giant is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The giant’s Stealth is 20 while in rocky terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant makes two Stony Fist or Rock attacks.

action.name: Stony Fist
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (4d8 + 6) bludgeoning damage. The target is grappled (escape DC 17) if it is a Large or smaller creature and the stone giant isn’t already grappling a creature.

action.name: Rock
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be knocked prone.

bonus.name: Wrestle
entries: The stone giant performs one of the following wrestling maneuvers against one creature it is grappling. The target must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or suffer the listed effect until the stone giant uses this bonus action again or until the grapple ends.
• Armbar. The target has disadvantage on attack rolls and
on checks made to escape the grapple.
• Chinlock. The target can’t speak and can’t cast spells
with verbal components.
• Takedown. The target is knocked prone and restrained.
• Throw. The target is thrown up to 30 feet in a random

direction and knocked prone, ending the grapple. If a thrown target strikes a solid surface, the target takes 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or take the same damage and be knocked prone.

reaction.name: Missile Deflection
entries: If a creature the giant can see makes a ranged weapon attack against the giant, the giant can add 3 to its AC. If the attack misses, the giant can catch the missile, provided the attack included a projectile, and throw it back at the attacker, using the giant’s normal ranged weapon attack bonus and the weapon’s normal damage. Giant, Storm A blue-skinned giantess reaches into the cloudy sky and pulls out a ball of lightning. Her long, white hair whips about her, crackling with electricity, as she squeezes the ball into a jagged, spear-like bolt of lightning moments before hurling it.

reaction.name: Lightning Lords
entries: Storm giants are masters of controlling and manipulating the energy of storms, pulling lightning from storm clouds to use as weapons. Though they can actively manipulate storms, nearby storms also respond to the giants’ moods, becoming a calm, sunny day when the giant is happy or a raging maelstrom when the giant is furious. When storm giants gather, their collective emotions drive the flow of the weather for miles around them, welcoming traveling merchants when the giants seek trade or toppling armies when the giants go to war.

reaction.name: Reclusive Ascetics
entries: Storm giants are aware of the effect their mood has on the local weather, and they often seek lives of solitude and calm reflection away from most other creatures. They live either alone or in small family groups in structures built on high coastal cliffs or remote tropical islands. They are noble folk, who are easy to befriend and quick to anger, especially if insulted or tricked.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The giant can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The giant is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Influence Weather
entries: Nearby weather responds to the storm giant’s desires. At the start of each minute, the storm giant can choose to change the precipitation, temperature, and wind within 3 miles of it by one stage, up or down (no action required). This effect works like the changing weather conditions aspect of the control weather spell, except the conditions change immediately and the storm giant can’t change the temperature by more than one stage up or down from the area’s standard temperature.

trait.name: Stormsight
entries: The storm giant can see through areas obscured by clouds, fog, rain, snow, and storms without penalty.

trait.name: Updraft
entries: While in an area open to the sky, the storm giant has a flying speed of 30 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant makes three Greatsword attacks, or it makes four Lightning Bolt attacks.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 30 (6d6 + 9) slashing damage.

action.name: Lightning Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d8 + 4) lightning damage. If the giant scores a critical hit, the target must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or be paralyzed until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Thunderous Strike (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The giant hurls a magical lightning bolt at a point it can see within 500 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must make a DC 17 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 36 (8d8) lightning damage and 21 (6d6) thunder damage and is stunned until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t stunned.

reaction.name: Stormy Rebuke
entries: When a creature the storm giant can see hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of the giant, the giant can send a stormy blast at the creature. The attacker must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the giant and knocked prone. Gibbering Mouther Fleshy extensions pull the eye-studded, amorphous mound across the ground as mind-rending burbling utters from its myriad mouths.

reaction.name: Absorb Prey
entries: Aggressively ravenous, gibbering mouthers exist to hunt and consume victims, absorbing their prey into their bodies and adding its eyes and mouths to theirs. A mouther’s chaotic whispering confuses and bewilders its next meal, allowing the slow aberration time to feed.

reaction.name: Agent of the Far Beyond
entries: Gibbering mouthers hail originally from chaotic planes, where they acted as passive scouts, spies, and guardians, allowing their masters to observe the world through their many eyes. Mouthers no longer have such connections and roam the planes untethered, consuming whatever they can catch.

reaction.name: Formless Body and Land
entries: The bodies of gibbering mouthers are collections of alien eyes and teeth-filled mouths suspended in shapeless blobs of oozing flesh. Though slow, mouthers’ otherworldly nature warps the land around them, making their prey easier to catch.

reaction.name: Maddening Murmurs
entries: The mouths of gibbering mouthers whisper a discordance of agonized voices, bestial sounds, and dark truths. Some claim these voices speak lost secrets, but most who listen for long risk losing their minds.

trait.name: Aberrant Ground
entries: The ground within 10 feet of the mouther is dough-like difficult terrain. A creature that starts its turn in the area must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or its speed is halved until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The mouther is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Gibbering
entries: The mouther babbles incoherently while it can see any creature and isn’t incapacitated. A creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the mouther and that can hear the gibbering must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or it can’t take reactions until the start of its next turn and must roll a d8 to determine what it does during its turn. On a 1 to 4, the creature does nothing. On a 5 or 6, the creature takes no action or bonus action and moves its full speed in a randomly determined direction. On a 7 or 8, the creature makes a melee attack against a randomly determined creature within its reach or does nothing if it can’t make such an attack.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gibbering mouther makes two Bite attacks. If both attacks hit one Medium or smaller creature, the target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Rend Mind
entries: One creature the mouther can see within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or take 18 (4d8) psychic damage and suffer a random, short-term dread effect for 1 minute

bonus.name: Blinding Spittle
entries: The mouther spits an otherworldly goo at up to two creatures it can see within 30 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 13 DEX save or be blinded until the end of its next turn. Gnoll This fierce, hyena-headed humanoid with mottled gray fur and a brown mane, barks a laugh, showing its sharp teeth as it raises a spear in clawed hands.

bonus.name: Grasslands Dwellers
entries: Gnoll packs roam the grasslands and deserts of the world, following herds of their favored prey and scavenging when all else fails. They live close to the land and the hyenas they resemble, and they generally keep themselves apart from most other humanoids. Some gnoll packs trade with the merchants that travel through their lands, some raid these merchants, and others hire themselves out as escorts and guards for such caravans.

bonus.name: Matriarchal Society
entries: Gnoll packs are typically made up of several matriarchal family units, and are, in turn, ruled over by the most powerful female warrior. This matriarch leads the pack’s warriors, and she is advised by each family’s leader along with an elder female oracle, who performs religious ceremonies and magical workings.

bonus.name: Rebellious Scions
entries: Gnolls have a wide variety of stories of their origins, but the one thing in common among all of them is that gnolls were once created as servitors to some otherworldly entity. These stories always include a gnoll matriarch leading her people against their masters, freeing the gnolls and sending them to a new life on the Material Plane. All gnolls retain a spark of independence and rebellion and are always ready to rise up against a tyrant.

trait.name: Heightened Smell
entries: The gnoll’s Perception is 15 when perceiving by smell.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Spear
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage, or 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

bonus.name: Taunting Bark
entries: The gnoll lets out a high-pitched laugh and taunts one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a DC 12 CHA save. On a failure, a creature takes 2 (1d4) psychic damage and must spend its next turn moving up to its speed toward the gnoll and make one attack against the gnoll.

reaction.name: Rampage
entries: When the gnoll reduces a creature to 0 HP , it can move up to half its speed and make one Bite attack. If it does, attack rolls against it have advantage until the start of its next turn. Gnome, Deep Pick in hand, this bald gnome with stony skin scrutinizes a large crystal for a moment. It then reaches into its apron and pulls out a crystal-topped tool and taps it on the larger crystal, which glows briefly in response.

reaction.name: Subterranean Isolationists
entries: Deep gnomes are small, subterranean humanoids distantly related to surface gnomes. They dwell in hidden communities, where they grow fungi for food. Deep gnomes tend to be standoffish and isolationist when it comes to interactions with outsiders, but once their trust is earned they can be steadfast allies.

reaction.name: Gem-Focused
entries: Deep gnomes have an affinity for gemstones and crystals, both mundane and magical. Subtle, innate magic within each gnome resonates with specific crystals, and their societies operate based on these talents, with each gnome contributing to the whole in a way unique to its resonance. Some resonances allow the gnomes to better sense particular crystals and gemstones hidden within the rock of their subterranean home, others allow the gnomes to generate light and other minor magics while holding the crystal, and a few allow the gnomes to grow and strengthen the crystals, creating tools, weapons, and even structures.

reaction.name: Elemental Relations
entries: Some deep gnomes resonate strongly with the primordial magic that holds earth elementals together. Because of this, many deep gnome settlements include several earth elemental citizens, who feel a kinship with such gnomes and help grow and defend the settlement. Conversely, the gnomes’ affinity for gemstones and crystals often puts them at odds with xorn, who eat such stones. More than one deep gnome crystalline statue, bridge, or building has collapsed from a group of hungry xorn gorging on the delicacy.

trait.name: Gnome Cunning
entries: The deep gnome has advantage on INT, WIS, and CHA saves against magic.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The deep gnome’s Stealth is 17 while in rocky terrain.

action.name: War Pick
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Crystalline Dart
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Infused Weapon
entries: The deep gnome crushes a magic-infused crystal on the end of its war pick. Until the start of the gnome’s next turn the war pick is magical and deals an extra 2 (1d4) force damage when the gnome hits with it.

bonus.name: Discordant Shatter
entries: The deep gnome throws a magic- infused crystal at a point it can see within 30 feet of it, shattering the crystal, which emits a rapid series of discordant waves of magic. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 12 CON save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn. A creature sensitive to or proficient in the use of magic, such as a spellcaster, has disadvantage on this save.

reaction.name: Crystalline Flash
entries: When a creature the deep gnome can see within 5 feet of it attacks it, it can throw a magic-infused crystal on the ground, shattering the crystal in a brilliant flash of light. If the attacker uses sight to perceive the gnome, the attacker must succeed on a DC 12 DEX save or have disadvantage on the attack roll. Goblins Stepping out of the shadows, this small, yellow-green humanoid has long, pointed ears, an upturned nose, and red eyes. It grins slyly as two larger figures step out of the shadows behind it. The brown-furred one raises a massive, heavily spiked club, while the other takes a practiced warrior’s stance and weighs its surroundings with shrewd, yellow eyes.

reaction.name: Varied and Fecund
entries: Goblins come in three distinct varieties: the sneaky goblin, the bullying bugbear, and the militaristic hobgoblin. Though the standard goblin is the most common, all have large families, and goblins typify the phrase “strength in numbers.” All goblins are opportunistic optimists, ignoring what others think of them, stubbornly prospering in badlands or inhospitable terrain, and finding the best in most situations. Goblins can find delight in digging a trinket out of the mud or stealing a shiny ring from a pompous human, while bugbears enjoy the screech of metal, snap of wood, and crunch of bones beneath their heavy clubs. Hobgoblins value order, such as in a job well done, a sharpened blade, or the taste of freshly-plundered ale.

reaction.name: Bullying Bugbears
entries: As the largest goblins, bugbears are named for their unique, furred and bearish appearance. They are more aggressive, destructive, and ferocious than their cousins. They bully their smaller cousins into obedience when possible. They don’t expect pushback, but they back down from the rare, coordinated response. The same happens when bugbears fight other creatures. If assured of an easy win, they use their size and strength to force opponents to back down. However, they flee when their opponents demonstrate superiority on the battlefield; only a powerful leader can push the bugbears to overcome their hesitation against a superior foe.

reaction.name: Thieving Goblins
entries: Goblins delight in the discovery of hidden things, and stealing hidden or protected items is an enjoyable challenge. T o goblins, anyone who can’t successfully guard their wealth doesn’t deserve it. Every goblin appreciates a good robbery, even if they are the victim. Many goblins parley their stealth, cunning, and practiced theft into careers. In cities, they excel in spycraft, information gathering, and thievery. In the wilderness, goblins are skillful scouts, trappers, and bandits.

reaction.name: Rule Bound Hobgoblins
entries: As a society, most hobgoblin enclaves are highly organized military bands, called cohorts, whose members are ranked by their skills and accomplishments. The militant hobgoblins live their lives according to the Decrees, a complicated set of rules, laws, and customs. All hobgoblins are sworn to serve their cohorts from birth, raised to honor both the Decrees and their superiors. The cohort combines military virtues with family loyalty, and to swear by the cohort is the most binding promise a hobgoblin can give.

trait.name: Brutal
entries: A melee weapon deals one extra die of its damage when the bugbear hits with it (included in the attack).

action.name: Spiked Club
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage. A surprised target takes an extra 4 (1d8) piercing damage and must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Javelin
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage in melee or 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage at range.

trait.name: Brutal
entries: A melee weapon deals one extra die of its damage when the bugbear hits with it (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bugbear champion makes two Spiked Club attacks and one Shield Smash attack, or it makes three Javelin attacks.

action.name: Spiked Club
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Shield Smash
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Javelin
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage in melee or 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage at range.

reaction.name: Stubborn Attacker (Recharge 5–6)
entries: When the champion misses with an attack, it can change that miss to a hit.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Gang Up (1/Day)
entries: The goblin moves up to half its speed toward a creature it can see. Each friendly goblin within 30 feet of the goblin can use its reaction to join the gang up and move up to half its speed toward the same target. This movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks. If the initiating goblin is within 5 feet of the target, the target must make a DC 12 DEX save, taking 5 (2d4) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. For each goblin after the first that participated in the gang up and that is within 10 feet of the target, the damage increases by 1 as arrows, knives, sharp pocket scraps, and similar “weapons” fly at the target from all angles. Afterwards, each goblin after the first that participated in the gang up can’t use Gang Up until it finishes a short or long rest.

bonus.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the Disengage or Hide action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin captain makes two Scimitar or Shortbow attacks. If both attacks hit one target, the goblin captain can make one extra Scimitar or Shortbow attack.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Text
entries: Get That One! The goblin captain points at a target and calls out to a friendly goblin it can see within 30 feet of it. The chosen goblin can use its reaction to move up to half its speed and make one melee attack against the target.

bonus.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the Disengage or Hide action.

reaction.name: Text
entries: Protect Me! When a creature the goblin captain can see targets it with an attack, the captain can sidestep behind an ally within 5 feet of it, moving to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the ally. The chosen ally becomes the target of the attack instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hobgoblin makes two Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) slashing damage, or 6 (1d10 + 1) slashing damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Longbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Tactical Analysis
entries: The hobgoblin briefly studies one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. It has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against that creature before the start of the hobgoblin’s next turn.

trait.name: Unshakable Command
entries: Each friendly creature within 30 feet of the hobgoblin commander can’t be charmed or frightened while the commander isn’t incapacitated.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hobgoblin commander makes three Greatsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Martial Tactics
entries: The hobgoblin commander employs one of the following tactics:
• Emboldening Shout. One friendly creature within 30
feet of the hobgoblin commander that it can see gains 7 (2d6) temporary HP until the start of the commander’s next turn.
• Pressing Advance. The commander moves up to half

its speed and commands one friendly creature it can see within 30 feet of it to also move. The target can use its reaction to move up to half its speed in the direction of the commander’s choosing. This movement for both creatures is unaffected by difficult terrain and doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hobgoblin conjurer makes two Conjurer’s Staff or Acid Bolt attacks. Conjurer’s Staff. Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Acid Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) acid damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The hobgoblin conjurer casts one of the following spells, using INT as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13).
At will: light, mage hand, prestidigitation
3/day each: fog cloud, grease
1/day: web

bonus.name: Harrying Specter
entries: The hobgoblin conjurer creates a spectral hobgoblin next to a creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or have disadvantage on attack rolls on its next turn.
