reaction.name: Rescuing Teleport
entries: When a bugbear, goblin, or hobgoblin takes damage while below half its HP maximum while within 30 feet of the conjurer, the conjurer can summon that creature to its side, provided the conjurer can see the target. The target teleports to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the conjurer. Golem, Clay A clay sculpture of a well-muscled dwarf steps off a plinth in the temple’s hallway, dwarven runes dancing in rings around its arms and neck. As it raises its arm, the clay of its hand shifts into an axe.

reaction.name: Divine Golems
entries: Any spellcaster with the power and knowledge is capable of creating a clay golem, but most clay golems are created by clerics and priests to protect high-ranking clergy, reliquaries, or particularly significant holy sites. The golems are usually sculpted to represent heroes within the faith’s stories, bearing the hero’s regal countenance, broad shoulders, infamous armor, or similar features. They are also sometimes painted with colors significant to their creator’s faith or colors said to be worn by the heroes they depict.

reaction.name: Sculpted Bodies
entries: Clay golems are almost always humanoid in shape, but the clay that makes up their bodies is somewhat moldable to the golem’s (or its creator’s) will, allowing the golem to shape its hands into different weapons. Similarly, clay golems’ personalities are moldable, and many develop personality quirks, likes, and dislikes reflective of the preferences of the clergy around them.

reaction.name: Creator Connection
entries: Like all golems, the clay golem contains something dear to its creator. Spellcasters not associated with temples often give drops of their blood during the creation process, while clergy often place a holy symbol, minor relic, or similar object or symbol significant to the faith at the heart of the golem.

monster.name: Clay Golem
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can’t speak

trait.name: Acid Absorption
entries: Whenever the golem is subjected to acid damage, it takes no damage and instead regains HP equal to the acid damage dealt.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The golem doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Fire Weakness
entries: When the clay golem takes fire damage, its body temporarily hardens like baked clay. Until the end of its next turn, the golem’s speed is reduced by 10 feet, it can’t use Mold Hand, and its AC becomes 18.

trait.name: Golem Resilience
entries: The golem is immune to poison and psychic damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem’s weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes three Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d10 + 5) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, as determined by Mold Hand. The target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or suffer an effect based on the type of damage: knocked prone (bludgeoning), pushed up to 10 feet away from the golem (piercing), or its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn (slashing).

action.name: Haste (Recharge 5–6)
entries: Until the end of its next turn, the golem magically gains a +2 bonus to its AC, has advantage on DEX saves, and can make a Slam attack as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Mold Hand
entries: The clay golem molds its hand into the shape of a weapon or back into a hand. The golem’s Slam attack deals bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, depending on the type of weapon chosen, until the golem uses this bonus action again. For example, a hand shaped like an axe deals slashing damage. Golem, Flesh The man steps forward with a lurching gait. The loose clothing covering his arms and face falls away, revealing a stitched- together body made up of disparate flesh.

bonus.name: Disparate Flesh
entries: Flesh golems are made of flesh from a variety of once-living humanoid creatures. This material typically limits the creation of flesh golems to spellcasters with knowledge of necromancy and requires a spellcaster to infuse the flesh with lightning to animate it.

bonus.name: Intelligent Golems
entries: Flesh golems are typically much smarter than other golems. Despite this, flesh golems are usually incapable of speech outside of basic words and phrases. The dead flesh that comprises them makes most muscular movement, like speech and walking, stilted. With a consciousness also comes the capability of fear, and flesh golems almost universally fear fire, understanding the danger it poses to flesh.

bonus.name: Creator Connection
entries: Flesh golems are almost always created by tormented or grieving spellcasters seeking to recreate or bring back a lost loved one. Because of this, almost all flesh golems contain within them some memento of the lost loved one, such as a child’s toy, a smoking pipe, a knitted scarf, or similar. The emotionally charged energy of these mementos leaves flesh golems fairly unstable, and many enter uncontrolled rages when in danger.

monster.name: Flesh Golem
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can’t speak

trait.name: Aversion of Fire
entries: If the golem takes fire damage, it loses its immunity to the frightened condition and has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Berserk (55 HP or Fewer)
entries: When the golem starts its turn, roll a d6. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object, with preference for an object smaller than itself. Once the golem goes berserk, it continues to do so until it is destroyed or regains all its HP . The golem’s creator, if within 60 feet of the berserk golem, can try to calm it by speaking firmly and persuasively. The golem must be able to hear its creator, who must take an action to make a DC 15 CHA (Persuasion) check. If the check succeeds, the golem ceases being berserk. If the golem takes damage while still at 55 HP or fewer, the golem might go berserk again.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The golem doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Flesh Golem Resilience
entries: The golem is immune to poison damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Lightning Absorption
entries: Whenever the golem is subjected to lightning damage, it takes no damage and instead regains HP equal to the lightning damage dealt.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem’s weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage. Creator’s Anguish (Recharge 5–6). The golem releases a wave of emotional anguish from its core. Each creature within 15 feet of the golem must make a DC 15 WIS save. On a failure, a creature takes 31 (9d6) psychic damage and is incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as its mind fills with memories of its greatest failures. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t incapacitated. Golem, Iron A large, metallic figure strides forward, its footfalls booming on the stone floor.

action.name: Forged Golems
entries: Each iron golem is forged by a team of blacksmiths led by a spellcaster. The process is grueling and taxing on all involved, and many iron golems never see completion, as smiths die of exhaustion or spellcasters tax their magic to the point of losing it.

action.name: Dungeon Guardians
entries: Iron golems produce poisonous gas within their bodies as a side effect of the creation process. Because of this quirk, they are particularly deadly in enclosed spaces and are often tasked with protecting dungeon corridors, underground prisons, and treasuries filled with valuables unaffected by poisonous gas.

action.name: Creator Connection
entries: Like all golems, the iron golem contains something dear to its creator. For iron golems, this is often a favored suit of armor, weapon, or shield that saved the creator’s life. This martial connection fills the golem, imbuing it with the fighting prowess of a dozen warriors.

monster.name: Iron Golem
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can’t speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The golem doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Golem Resilience
entries: The golem is immune to poison and psychic damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Fire Absorption
entries: Whenever the golem is subjected to fire damage, it takes no damage and instead regains HP equal to the fire damage dealt.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem’s weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Warrior Stance
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the iron golem chooses one of the following fighting stances to adopt, which lasts until the start of the golem’s next turn.
• Aggressive. When the golem uses Multiattack, it can
replace one additional Spear Arm attack with a Shield Bash attack. If it does so, its AC is reduced by 2 until the start of its next turn.
• Retributive. The next time the golem is hit by a melee

weapon attack from a creature within 5 feet of it before the start of its next turn, the golem can slam its shoulder into the attacker as a reaction. The attacker must succeed on a DC 18 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the golem and knocked prone.
• Defensive. The golem’s AC increases by 4, but it can’t
make Shield Bash attacks until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes four Spear Arm attacks. It can replace one Spear Arm attack with a Shield Bash attack.

action.name: Shield Bash
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 29 (4d10 + 7) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 18 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Spear Arm
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 25 (4d8 + 7) piercing damage. Expel Poisonous Gas (Recharge 5–6). The golem vents poisonous gas from small holes across its body. Each creature within 15 feet of the golem must make a DC 18 CON save, taking 54 (12d8) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Golem, Stone A perched bird suddenly takes flight as the overgrown pile of rocks where it perched rises to reveal a large, human-like collection of stone.

action.name: Repurposed Golems
entries: Any stone can be used to create a stone golem, but most such golems are made from reclaimed stone that has outlived its original purpose. Built from ancient rock, the golem perceives the passage of each breath, each raindrop, and each beam of sunlight and can briefly share this perception with others.

action.name: Druidic Creations
entries: Any spellcaster with the power and knowledge is capable of creating a stone golem, but most stone golems are created by druids seeking to protect the natural world. These golems are covered in vines, mosses, guano, and lichen.

action.name: Creator Connection
entries: Like all golems, the stone golem contains something dear to its creator. Stone golems created in urban environments by arcane spellcasters often include a building’s cornerstone painted with arcane symbols of protection in the creator’s blood. Stone golems created in forests by druids often contain heirloom seeds, petrified branches, or other symbols significant to the druid’s order.

monster.name: Stone Golem
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can’t speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The golem doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Golem Resilience
entries: The golem is immune to poison and psychic damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to exhaustion and to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem’s weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes three Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Slow (Recharge 6)
entries: The golem shares the plodding perception of timeless stone with those nearby, slowing all life to a crawl. Each creature within 10 feet of the golem must make a DC 17 WIS save. On a failure, a creature can’t use reactions, its speed is halved, and it can’t make more than one attack on its turn. In addition, the creature can take either an action or a bonus action on its turn, not both. These effects last for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Rearrange Stone
entries: The stone golem rearranges some of the stone comprising its body, choosing one of the following arrangements. The arrangement lasts until the golem ends the effect as a bonus action, uses this bonus action again, or until it is incapacitated.
• Jagged Stones. The golem turns several of the stones
on its body to point outward. Its AC is reduced by 2, and each time a creature within 5 feet of the golem hits it with a melee attack, the attacker takes 5 (2d4) piercing damage.
• Protective Slabs. The golem slides several flat stones

around its body to protect its vulnerable areas. Its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and each time it takes damage, the damage is reduced by 5 (2d4). Gorgon The stench of the creature’s breath comes first, a sulfuric and choking miasma. The creature exhaling this green vapor is a bull-like beast completely covered in overlapping iron plates. It paws at the ground with mighty hooves and lowers wickedly sharp horns for the attack.

bonus.name: Uncertain Origins
entries: Studies differ as to the origins of the gorgons. Some claim they were created by mages to act as guardians, others name deities of the forge or metal as the originator. T oday, gorgons roam grasslands, scrubland, and hills, and they propagate like any other living creature.

bonus.name: Obsessively Territorial
entries: A gorgon herd consists of an older bull gorgon, several females, and younger males. Gorgon herds prefer wide, open spaces and frequent mineral outcroppings. They guard their range fiercely, seldom tolerating any creature larger than a hare.

bonus.name: Poisonous Gullet
entries: The gorgon prefers to feed on grass and herbs, but it also feasts on shards of obsidian, pyrite, and cinnabar chipped from natural deposits by its horns. In addition to helping the gorgon digest the vegetation it eats, the minerals also break down in the gorgon’s stomach, producing the gorgon’s legendary gas.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The gorgon is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the gorgon moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the gorgon can make one Hooves attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gorgon makes one Gore attack and one Hooves attack.

action.name: Gore
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d12 + 5) piercing damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Petrifying Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The gorgon exhales poisonous, petrifying gas in a 30-foot cone. Creatures in the area must make a DC 13 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 21 (6d6) poison damage and is restrained as it begins to turn to stone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t restrained. A restrained creature must repeat the save at the end of its next turn. On a success, the effect ends on the creature. On a failure, the creature is petrified until freed by the greater restoration spell or other magic.

reaction.name: Poisonous Retort
entries: When the gorgon is hit by an attack from a creature it can see within 10 feet of it, the gorgon can snort angrily, releasing a puff of poisonous gas at the creature. The attacker must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be poisoned until the end of its next turn. Gray Ooze Fresh mortar glistens on the stone wall. Suddenly, the mortar rises up, snake-like, and lashes out.

reaction.name: Living Mortar
entries: The first gray oozes appeared when curing mortar was accidentally exposed to excess magic near a building site. The oozes have since propagated into their own type of ooze, though some still form when curing mortar is exposed to strong magic. Comprised of the caustic materials that make up mortar, gray oozes can dissolve most material other than stone, but they subsist primarily on animals they can catch.

reaction.name: Stone Affinity
entries: Perhaps due to their origins as mortar, gray oozes have an affinity for stone, particularly worked stone. They are often found in dungeons, stone cities, or old brick buildings, and some longer-lived gray oozes develop minor bonds with the stone they frequent, becoming territorial and protective of it.

reaction.name: Camouflaged Ambushers
entries: When the putty-like ooze lies dormant, a thin crust forms across the surface of its body, making it look like freshly laid mortar. Using this, gray oozes often stretch themselves across worked stone and hide as ordinary mortar to ambush prey.

monster.name: Gray Ooze
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Corrode Metal
entries: Any nonmagical weapon made of metal that hits the ooze corrodes. After dealing damage, the weapon takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to −5, the weapon is destroyed. Nonmagical ammunition made of metal that hits the ooze is destroyed after dealing damage. The ooze can eat through 2-inch-thick, nonmagical metal in 1 round.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the ooze remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from ordinary fresh or curing mortar.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The ooze doesn’t require sleep.

trait.name: Ooze Resilience
entries: The ooze is resistant to the grappled and restrained conditions, and it is immune to exhaustion and to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, and prone conditions.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ooze can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The grick makes one Beak attack and four Tentacle attacks.

action.name: Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 15). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, the grick can automatically hit the target with its Beak, and the grick can’t make Beak attacks against other targets.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Solidify
entries: When the ooze is hit by an attack, it can partially solidify its body, reducing the damage it takes by 2 (1d4). If it has taken fire damage since the start of its last turn, the damage it takes is reduced by 3 (1d6) instead. The ooze can’t use this reaction if it has taken cold damage since the start of its last turn. Immune charmed Senses darkvision 60 ft. Languages — STR DEX CON INT WIS CHA +4 +3 +2 −3 +2 −1 Coordinated Strikes. Each friendly grick within 10 feet of the advanced grick has advantage on attack rolls against any creature the advanced grick has attacked since the start of its last turn.

reaction.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: As grick.

reaction.name: Spider Climb
entries: As grick.

reaction.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The grick’s Stealth is 18 while in rocky terrain.

bonus.name: Body Slam
entries: The grick slams its body into a creature it can see within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the grick and knocked prone. Grick The large, beaked worm uncurls from the stone wall and lashes out with tentacles ending in long barbs.

bonus.name: Underground Predators
entries: Gricks are subterranean predators that wander the caverns and dungeons of the world. Their orange skin shifts to gray to blend into their stony surroundings as they hunt prey.

bonus.name: Grick Gaps
entries: Though solitary hunters, gricks cohabitate with other gricks in communal lairs called gaps. Any prey not consumed by a hunting grick is often dragged back to the gap for the benefit of all.

bonus.name: Grick Leaders
entries: When prey is plentiful, a particularly large and powerful grick might hatch in a gap. These advanced gricks have additional tentacles around their beaks to better hold prey. They are natural leaders, coordinating small groups of gricks on hunts to take down larger prey.

trait.name: Coordinated Strikes
entries: Each friendly grick within 10 feet of the advanced grick has advantage on attack rolls against any creature the advanced grick has attacked since the start of its last turn.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: As grick.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: As grick.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The grick’s Stealth is 18 while in rocky terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The grick makes one Beak attack and four Tentacle attacks.

action.name: Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 15). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, the grick can automatically hit the target with its Beak, and the grick can’t make Beak attacks against other targets.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Body Slam
entries: The grick slams its body into a creature it can see within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the grick and knocked prone.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The grick is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The grick can climb difficult surfaces, including upside ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The grick’s Stealth is 17 in rocky terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The grick makes one Beak attack and one Tentacles attack.

action.name: Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Tentacles
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (4d4 + 2) piercing damage.

trait.name: Heightened Sight
entries: The griffon’s Perception is 18 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The griffon is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The griffon has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the griffon’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the griffon moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claws attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the griffon can make one Beak attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The griffon makes one Beak attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage. Grimlock Uttering a repetitive clicking sound, a rugged, human-like creature shuffles forward, cocking its head to one side as if listening intently. It has gray skin, stringy hair, and large, sightless white eyes.

action.name: Ancient Humans
entries: Grimlocks descend from the human population of a vast empire long ago lost to the underground in some forgotten calamity. Over generations, they adapted to the world below ground, gradually losing their sight and evolving new senses. Relying on hearing and scent, grimlocks thrive in dark environments. However, their extremely sensitive ears are particularly vulnerable to loud sounds.

action.name: Echosense
entries: Grimlocks speak and understand the language of the subterranean world, though they prefer to speak their own dialect of Dwarvish. The clicking sounds that form most of their words also serve as a form of echolocation.

action.name: Blind Stewards
entries: Xenophobic hunter-gatherers, grimlocks retain some slight vestige of their ancestors’ mighty empire in the form of complex techno-arcane devices. Grimlock shamans use oral traditions to pass down knowledge of how to maintain these machines, though few grimlocks know how to construct them. This doesn’t stop grimlocks from pocketing baubles cast off from these devices, and many grimlocks have found the baubles to be effective at repelling or disorienting intruders.

action.name: Accidental Transformation
entries: Occasionally, regular exposure to some of the larger techno-arcane devices of their ancestors will mutate and transform an unsuspecting grimlock. This transformation increases the grimlock’s size and strength, removes much of the grimlock’s hair, and fills the grimlock with a magical, red light that shines from its eyes and gives its stony, gray skin a sandy hue. T ransformed grimlocks are known as morlocks among their people and are often relied on as defenders of grimlock settlements. Unfortunately, the transformation and whatever magic now imbues the morlock greatly shortens its lifespan. Many grimlock shamans have spent lifetimes studying morlocks, seeking a cure, a way to recreate the process, or a way to extend the lifespans of their transformed kin.

action.name: Magical Morlocks
entries: Each morlock’s body holds raw magic from the techno-arcane device that transformed it. This magic gives morlocks some protection against magic, but the magic also eats at the morlock, shortening its lifespan and causing it near-constant discomfort. T o ease this discomfort, morlocks occasionally vent this magic in short bursts. When defending their homes, morlocks use this necessary venting to also hinder their foes.

monster.name: Grimlock
senses: keensense 30 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The grimlock’s Perception is 16 when perceiving by hearing or smell.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The grimlock’s Stealth is 18 while in rocky terrain.

trait.name: Suppressed Senses
entries: The grimlock can’t use its keensense while deafened and unable to smell.

action.name: Spiked Club
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Strange Bauble
entries: The grimlock reaches into a pocket, pulls out a small object, and throws it at a creature the grimlock can sense within 30 feet. The grimlock chooses one of the following objects:
• Flashing Rock. The grimlock throws a warm, scintillating
rock at the target that bursts with a myriad of colors. The target must succeed on a DC 11 DEX save or be blinded until the end of its next turn.
• Illusory Dancer. The grimlock throws a small disk that
emits a blurry, fractured illusion of a graceful, humanoid dancer. The target must succeed on a DC 11 CHA save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn as it is mesmerized by the dance.
• Whirling Death. The grimlock throws a small, bladed

gear that grows larger and larger as it travels toward the target, threatening to slice the target into pieces. The target must succeed on a DC 11 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target realizes the gear’s growth was a magical, illusory effect and that the gear never increased in size.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The morlock’s Perception is 15 when perceiving by hearing or smell.

trait.name: Magic-Infused Blood
entries: A creature that hits the morlock with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 2 (1d4) force damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The morlock has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Suppressed Senses
entries: The morlock can’t use its keensense while deafened and unable to smell.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (4d6 +5) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Eject Magic
entries: The morlock expels some of the magic built up within its body, choosing one of the following effects. It can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row.
• Arrhythmic Burst. The morlock claws at its breastbone

then arches its back in pain, releasing a burst of magical energy that thrums like a heartbeat. Each creature concentrating on a spell within 30 feet of the morlock must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or lose concentration on the spell. Each creature within 5 feet of the morlock must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as its heartrate briefly becomes arrhythmic, matching the thrum of magic.
• Hacking Cough. The morlock coughs up a small, noxious

cloud. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
• Timelost Screech. The morlock opens its mouth and

exhales the sound of hundreds of ancient metal devices screeching to life at once in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be deafened and frightened until the end of its next turn. Grimlocks automatically succeed on the save. Hag, Ambush The illusion fades, revealing a distorted figure with one too many joints in its arms and legs. Huge, glossy black eyes shine over a slitted nose, and the cruel smile opens into spiderlike mandibles.

bonus.name: Helpless Feast
entries: Ambush hags subsist on the helplessness of trapped or solitary creatures. They construct seemingly welcome environments, such as a warm and cozy cottage in the wilderness, to lure victims. Beneath this pleasant illusion, their nest is filled with traps to capture and immobilize prey. As their fate slowly overwhelms them, their despair feeds the hidden hag.

bonus.name: Expert Illusionists
entries: Ambush hags can climb on walls, and they produce a sticky, paralytic poison. T o avoid scaring away prey, the hag uses illusions to appear as a kindly, older woman who needs assistance “just over there” or some similar ruse. Some ambush hags hide their presence altogether, relying on the lure of their well- crafted nests, filled with the scent of baking bread or warm candlelight.

bonus.name: Unusual Allies
entries: While ambush hags have an affinity for spiders and regularly work with them, many also work with mimics, who can reinforce the illusion of comfort and safety. A mimic keeps prey captured and can feast on the remains once the hag has had its fill of helplessness and despair. In return, the mimic gets shelter and assistance in luring prey closer.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The ambush hag is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The hag can mimic Animal sounds and Humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful DC 16 WIS (Insight) check.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ambush hag can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ambush hag makes two Claw attacks or three Poison Spray attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage plus 18 (4d8) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Poison Spray
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +8 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d8 + 4) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be restrained until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The ambush hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16):
At will: message, minor illusion, prestidigitation
3/day each: blur, charm, hallucinatory terrain (as an action)
1/day each: hypnotic pattern, major image, seeming

bonus.name: Illusory Appearance
entries: The hag covers itself and anything it is wearing or carrying with a magical illusion that makes it look like another creature of its general size and humanoid shape. The illusion ends if the hag takes a bonus action to end it or if the hag dies. The changes wrought by this effect fail to hold up to physical inspection. For example, the hag could appear to have smooth skin, but someone touching it would feel its rough flesh. Otherwise, a creature must take an action to visually inspect the illusion and succeed on a DC 20 INT (Investigation) check to discern that the hag is disguised. Hag, Green The sneering, hideous face of this sickly, green crone peers from beneath a tangle of long, gray hair.

bonus.name: Revel in Pain
entries: Green hags are fey that enjoy causing emotional suffering, subsisting on the psychic energy of the resulting pain. Cloaked in illusions, green hags visit humanoid settlements, where they learn the people and use that knowledge to cause suffering, spreading lies and manipulating people into vicious slander, false accusations, theft, infidelity, and even murder.

bonus.name: Deep Forests and Swamps
entries: Green hags live in dismal, overgrown lands, such as haunted forests, festering swamps, and overgrown moors. Green hags most often keep guardian snakes and pet spiders, finding them more agreeable than other creatures.

bonus.name: Wisdom for a Price
entries: Of all hags, green hags associate with humanoids the most. T ales of witches, wicked elders, and enchanters in the woods often come from a nearby green hag. Some even act as wise elders living openly in the woods outside of humanoid settlements, where they offer medicinal cures and wisdom—for a price. Unfortunately, the cure may not always be what the petitioner expects.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The hag can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The hag is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The hag can mimic Animal sounds and Humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful DC 14 WIS (Insight) check.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts
entries: The hag can communicate with Beasts as if they shared a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hag makes two Claw or Torment attacks. If two attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 14 WIS save or be overwhelmed with pain and have disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Torment
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) psychic damage. Poisonous Allies (1/Day). The hag magically calls 1 giant spider, 2 giant poisonous snakes, or 2d4 poisonous snakes (see Animals Appendix for statistics). They arrive in 1d4 rounds, acting as allies of the hag and obeying its spoken commands. The creatures remain for 1 hour, until the hag dies, or until the hag dismisses them as a bonus action.

action.name: Invisible Passage
entries: The hag magically turns invisible until it attacks or casts a spell, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). While invisible, it leaves no physical evidence of its passage and can be tracked only by magic. Any equipment it wears or carries is invisible with it.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14).
At will: dancing lights, minor illusion
1/day: bestow curse

bonus.name: Illusory Appearance
entries: The hag covers itself and anything it is wearing or carrying with a magical illusion that makes it look like another creature of its general size and Humanoid shape. The illusion ends if the hag takes a bonus action to end it or if the hag dies. The changes wrought by this effect fail to hold up to physical inspection. For example, the hag could appear to have smooth skin, but someone touching it would feel its rough flesh. Otherwise, a creature must take an action to visually inspect the illusion and succeed on a DC 20 INT (Investigation) check to discern that the hag is disguised. 2 11 HH Hag, Night The red eyes of this withered, violet-skinned crone bore into all it perceives as its lips peel back in a hideous, fanged grin.

bonus.name: Fiendish Hags
entries: When a green hag spends decades of its life on the Lower Planes, it often transforms into a night hag. Some green hags seek this transformation and revel in the power it brings. Others see the transformation as a corruption of the hags’ connection to the natural world and work to cure or cull their tainted sisters.

bonus.name: Feast on Sorrow
entries: Like all hags, night hags thrive on distress in mortal creatures. For night hags, this is sorrow. Night hags often hide in the Ethereal Plane and cause nightmares that leave a victim weeping in its sleep, and they regularly sup on the little, daily occurrences that weigh on the mortal soul, causing it to sink deeper into depression.

bonus.name: Soul Brokers
entries: Night hags are able to taint souls for the Lower Planes, usually by inflicting nightmares on sleeping mortals. A night hag gains these souls when the creature dies and uses them either as currency in the Lower Planes or as rewards to fiends working for the hag.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The hag has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The night hag can use Cause Sorrow. It then makes two Claw or Arcane Bolt attacks. It can replace one Claw or Arcane Bolt attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw (Hag Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Arcane Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d10 + 3) force damage.

action.name: Cause Sorrow
entries: One creature the hag can see within 10 feet of it must succeed on a DC 14 CHA save or be filled with sorrow for 1 minute. While filled with sorrow, a creature is incapacitated and experiences visions or memories of sorrowful events. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns (if conscious) or end of each hour (if unconscious), ending the effect on itself on a success. For each hour an unconscious creature is affected, its HP maximum is reduced by 5 (1d10). This reduction lasts until removed by the greater restoration spell or similar magic. The creature dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0. If an unconscious creature suffers from Cause Sorrow for at least 1 hour, it doesn’t gain any benefits from the rest. The hag can have only one creature filled with sorrow at a time. If it uses Cause Sorrow on another, the effect on the previous creature ends.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14).
At will: ray of enfeeblement, silent image
2/day each: detect thoughts, plane shift (self only), sleep

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The hag magically transforms into Medium or smaller Humanoid, or back into its true form, which is Fiend. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Etherealness
entries: The hag magically enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. The hag can use Cause Sorrow on an unconscious creature not on its plane; otherwise, the hag can’t affect or be affected by anything on the other plane. Hag, Sea Limp, kelp-like hair drapes the body of this withered crone, not quite hiding her sagging, green skin or the slimy patches of scales.

bonus.name: Coastal Fey
entries: Sea hags are aquatic fey that live along coastlines, often in marshy coves, barren sea caves, or shipwrecks. They often ally with other coastal sea creatures, such as sahuagin and selkie.

bonus.name: Devour Fear
entries: Sea hags subsist on the fear emitted by mortal creatures. They enjoy playing tricks on sailors, travelers, and other land-dwelling creatures to make them fear the sea and the creatures that lurk in it. Many tales of frightening creatures along the coast can be attributed to decades of the subtle machinations of a sea hag.

bonus.name: Purposefully Hideous
entries: Sea hags believe the tastiest fear comes from vanity and the fear of losing beauty. They grow slimy scales across their body, leave their hair and clothing unkempt, and even collect patches of barnacles on their skin to display themselves as horrific examples of bodies left to the sea. They show beautiful, vain humanoids their own decay to imply that every creature’s beauty will wither and fade.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The sea hag can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The sea hag is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Horrific Appearance
entries: When a Humanoid that can see the hag’s true form starts its turn within 30 feet of the hag, the hag can force it to make a DC 12 WIS save. On a failure, the creature is frightened until the start of its next turn. Unless surprised or the revelation of the hag’s true form is sudden, such as the ending of Illusory Appearance, a creature can avert its eyes to avoid the save at the start of its turn. If the creature does so, it can’t see the hag until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If the creature looks at the hag in the meantime, it must immediately make the save.

trait.name: Underwater Camouflage
entries: The hag’s Stealth is 18 while underwater and not using Illusory Appearance.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hag uses Death Glare. It then makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Death Glare
entries: One creature the hag can see within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 12 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its next turn. If the target is already frightened and fails the save, it is reduced to 0 HP instead. If reduced to 0 HP in this way, the creature is stable.

bonus.name: Illusory Appearance
entries: The hag covers itself and anything it is wearing or carrying with a magical illusion that makes it look like another creature of its general size and Humanoid shape. The illusion ends if the hag takes a bonus action to end it or if the hag dies. The changes from this effect fail to hold up to physical inspection. For example, the hag could appear to have smooth skin, but someone touching it would feel its scaly flesh. Otherwise, a creature must take an action to visually inspect the illusion and succeed on a DC 20 INT (Investigation) check to discern that the hag is disguised.

reaction.name: Barnacle Block
entries: When the hag takes damage from a source it can see, the hag can shift the barnacles and scales on its body to absorb some of the impact, reducing the damage by 4 (1d8). Harpy With a human body and the wings and talons of a swift raptor, this creature slashes the air as it dives at prey.

reaction.name: Coastal Aeries
entries: Harpies dwell in dark forests and along coastlines, building their aeries in high places to protect their young. They live in large flocks of six to ten mated pairs and their children. A typical clamor of harpies is led by the strongest hunters or most powerful singers. Dangerous hunters with a taste for human and giant flesh, they lure prey to its doom using their magical voices or descend upon prey with talon and claw.

reaction.name: Agents of the Divine
entries: Harpies were favored messengers of the gods until some ancient transgression exiled them from the divine realms. Most harpies have no stories or legends of that time, but a few clamors of harpies retain fragments of divine prophecies, rituals, or messages they carried long ago. Some harpies still seek to regain their status as messengers, while others work actively against all gods, embittered by the loss of their status.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The harpy is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The harpy makes two Claw or Screech attacks. If both Screech attacks hit one Giant or Humanoid, the target has disadvantage on the next save it makes against the harpy’s Luring Song before the start of the harpy’s next turn.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Screech
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d6 + 1) thunder damage.

bonus.name: Luring Song
entries: The harpy sings a magical melody. Every Humanoid and Giant within 300 feet of the harpy that can hear the song must succeed on a DC 11 WIS save or be charmed until the song ends. The harpy must use a bonus action on its subsequent turns to continue singing. It can stop singing at any time. The song ends if the harpy is incapacitated. While charmed by the harpy, a target is incapacitated and ignores the songs of other harpies. If the charmed target is more than 5 feet away from the harpy, the target must move on its turn toward the harpy by the most direct route, trying to get within 5 feet. It doesn’t avoid opportunity attacks, but before moving into damaging terrain, such as lava or a pit, and whenever it takes damage from a source other than the harpy, the target can repeat the save. A charmed target can also repeat the save at the end of each of its turns. If the save is successful, the effect ends on it. A target that successfully saves is immune to this harpy’s song for the next 24 hours. Hell Hound Brimstone permeates the air around this hulking canine. The beast’s claws are curved daggers, its eyes shine with a burning radiance, and flames curl out of its fanged mouth.

bonus.name: Wild Fiends
entries: Few know the origins of hell hounds, but all agree they are dangerous fiends and just as common in the Lower Planes as fire. Though hell hounds can and do serve more powerful fiends, they are wild at heart and hate to serve for too long.

bonus.name: Infernal Guardians
entries: Hell hounds often protect sites important to demons or devils, chasing off trespassers and raising the alarm to call more powerful fiends. Greater demons and devils occasionally reward mortals with the service of hell hounds, relying on the hound to aid the supplicant but also report back about their actions.

bonus.name: No Mere Beast
entries: Despite their animal shape and bestial instincts, hell hounds are cunning and often cruel fiends. Opponents who expect them to act like hounds discover the error before being consumed. Hell hounds prefer to fight in great numbers, and when they begin a hunt, very little dissuades them.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The hound has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hound’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Prey Sense
entries: The hound can pinpoint, by scent, the location of Medium and smaller creatures within 30 feet of it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hell hound makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

action.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The hound exhales fire in a 15- foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 DEX save, taking 21 (6d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Hippocampus This sea creature has the foreparts of a horse, though with a dorsal fin rather than a mane. Its hind quarters are a large fish tail.

action.name: Horses of the Sea
entries: These large herbivores live in herds of eight to twenty in coastal waters, feeding mainly on kelp and underwater plants. About the size of a riding horse, their bodies are covered in fine scales, ranging in color from white like sea foam, to deep blues and greens. Hippocampi that live and breed among merfolk or selkies often grow larger, comparable to draft horses in size.

action.name: Coastal Roamers
entries: Hippocampus herds prefer shallow, warmer waters where large predators are scarce. They roam deeper and colder areas for food and retreat to warmer and safer bays and coves at nightfall.

action.name: Humanoid Affinity
entries: Hippocampi often live with aquatic humanoids. Merfolk, selkies, and aquatic elves and gnomes ride them as mounts or hitch them to barges or sea chariots to carry goods. This arrangement suits the creatures well, because the hippocampi can lay eggs and raise their young in safety. In many areas, hippocampi save land-dwelling humanoids from drowning after shipwrecks, driving off sharks, or rescue fishing boats from sahuagin or sea hags.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The hippocampus can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Shared Breath
entries: The hippocampus’s gills pull more air from the water than the hippocampus needs, expelling the excess as bubbles down its neck and across its back. If an air-breathing creature is riding the hippocampus, these bubbles can provide that creature a supply of fresh air when the hippocampus isn’t in combat or traveling at a fast pace.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hippocampus makes one Bite attack and one Tail Slap attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Tail Slap
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Rearing Kick
entries: The hippocampus rears up and kicks its forelegs at one creature it can see within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone or pushed up to 10 feet away from the hippocampus (the hippocampus’s choice). Hippogriff Flashing down from the heavens, this creature has the body and hind legs of a horse but the head, wings, and forelegs of a mighty eagle. With a raptor’s scream, it dives to strike.

bonus.name: Foothills Hunter
entries: The swift hippogriffs hunt the mountains, foothills, plains, and wild places of the world. They are omnivorous but prefer meat. A single hippogriff covers hundreds of square miles as its hunting territory, and some of them follow herd animals as they migrate.

bonus.name: Eternal Mates
entries: Hippogriffs mate for life, raising one to two young at a time. They lay eggs, taking turns watching the hatchlings while the other mate hunts for food. When a hippogriff dies, its mate often starves itself to death.

bonus.name: Loyal Steeds
entries: Hippogriff eggs are prized treasures and valuable to everyone from elven lords to powerful human generals. Of all the monstrous mounts, hippogriffs are the easiest to train. However, the death of a hippogriff ’s rider sends the mount into a frenzy.

trait.name: Heightened Sight
entries: The hippogriff’s Perception is 18 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The hippogriff is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hippogriff makes one Beak attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Dive (Recharge 5–6)
entries: While flying, the hippogriff dives onto a creature below it. The hippogriff moves at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a creature it can see. The target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or take 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. Hivebound A reptilian creature flies on shimmering wings, always surrounded by the drone of insects. A beehive covers its head, as if grown there, and it exhales a swarm of angry bees.

bonus.name: Symbiotic History
entries: Hivebound are the result of a centuries-ago symbiotic relationship between the reptilian obidia and the chatta bees they farmed. Chatta honey held psychic energies that transferred to anyone who ate it, making them temporarily bond with the hive and protect it. Over time, the obidia ate more and more honey, until eventually the two became one being. The obidia’s intelligence infused the hive, elevating it above other insects, and the hive filled the obidia’s body.

bonus.name: Whole Being
entries: Hivebound are unique creatures made up of a hive inside a reptilian body. They propagate by sending out a young queen with a small, egg-like piece of the hive that contains an incubating reptilian body. When it hatches, the queen climbs into the mouth, bonds with the mindless body to establish the hive mind, and begins creating the rest of the hive’s members.

bonus.name: Other Hives
entries: Though hivebound started with obidia and chatta bees, they have since expanded to include other types of insects that create colonies or hives, such as ants, termites, and wasps. Hivebound often control swarms of other insects that have fallen under their sway.

monster.name: Hivebound
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Collective Mind
entries: The hivebound’s individual insects and swarms under its control are connected via a hive mind. It can telepathically communicate with any of its individual insects and swarms within 1 mile of it, and it can’t be surprised. It can have up to five swarms of insects under its control at a time.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The hivebound is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Reinforced Mind
entries: If the hivebound starts its turn afflicted by an ongoing effect that allows a repeated INT, WIS, or CHA save to end the effect, such as the hold spell, the hivebound can make the save at the start of the turn instead of the end and has advantage on the save.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hivebound makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks, or it makes three Spit Insect attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) poison damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Spit Insect
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be pestered until the end of its next turn. A pestered creature has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the pester ends.

action.name: Swarm Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The hivebound exhales stinging insects in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 10 (4d4) piercing damage and 17 (5d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A swarm of insects under the hivebound’s control then forms in an unoccupied space in that area.

bonus.name: Swift Flight
entries: The hivebound flies up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks. Homunculus This diminutive creature has the wings of a bat and fingers tipped in black claws. Its voice is screechy and thin.

bonus.name: Arcane Servants
entries: The first homunculi were made by arcanists seeking to create life via magical or alchemical means. T oday, many spellcasters create homunculi as spies, assistants, messengers, and even sneakthieves. Homunculi are proud of their status and connection to their masters, and they often view themselves as true assistants, not servants, and clearly superior to any other familiar.

bonus.name: All That Glitters
entries: Homunculi have an unending fascination with magical auras and shiny objects, a fascination inherited from their creators’ innate interest in magic. A homunculus will touch and handle any glittering object unless its master explicitly forbids it, and many will hoard small shiny objects in out of the way roosts.

bonus.name: Bonded to Creator
entries: The link between master and homunculus is akin to sharing the same mind. Knowledge is shared between them with a thought, and the master’s desires rarely need be spoken aloud. A spellcaster that loses a homunculus suffers a brief melancholy and a temporary disruption of their magic. A homunculus that loses its creator enters a deep melancholy, especially if the loss was sudden. Homunculi that survive this period of melancholy often seek new spellcasters for bonding within a matter of days or weeks.

monster.name: Homunculus
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can’t speak

trait.name: Bound Construct
entries: The homunculus serves another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded and on the same plane of existence, the companion can sense what the homunculus senses and the two can communicate telepathically. While the homunculus is within 100 feet of the companion, it can use a reaction to deliver any spell the companion casts that has a range of touch. If the spell requires an attack roll, the homunculus uses the companion’s spell attack modifier for the roll. While the homunculus is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion has advantage on INT (Arcana) and INT (Investigation) checks. At any time and for any reason, the homunculus can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The homunculus doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The homunculus is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 12 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. If the save fails by 5 or more, the target is instead poisoned for 5 (1d10) minutes and unconscious while poisoned in this way. UNATTACHED HOMUNCULI Almost all homunculi serve spellcasters, especially those that utilize arcane magic. However, some homunculi become unattached from their spellcasters, most often when that spellcaster dies. Such homunculi tend to seek out new spellcasters to form a new bond and attempt to entice potential masters withcollected shiny objects. A homunculus not bonded to a spellcaster loses the Bound Construct trait and has disadvantage on attack rolls and saves until it spends a long rest bonding with a willing spellcaster. Hydra The creature’s twisting, serpentine body branches into five necks, each ending in a scaled head with snapping jaws.

action.name: Fixated Guardians
entries: Hydras are extremely territorial lesser dragons, charging aggressively at any threat or potential prey. They are primarily solitary beasts concerned only with hunting, but they occasionally become fixated on a creature, object, structure, or natural terrain feature. A fixated hydra stays near the obsession, protecting it for as long as there is food nearby.

action.name: Multiheaded Horrors
entries: When first encountered, most hydras have five heads. They can regenerate a missing head with two new ones almost instantly, allowing them to continue hunting regardless of the danger. Mysteriously, hydras with more than five heads lose the extra heads within a day; the extras wither and fall away or (if the hydra is starving) are consumed by the remaining heads.

action.name: Wetland Predators
entries: Hydras dwell in warm swamps and marshes, though some inhabit fish-filled lakes in more temperate climes. Hydras are the top predators in their wetland homes. Only something as powerful as an adult black dragon or a concerted effort by a large collection of humanoids can drive a hydra to seek new hunting territory.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The hydra can hold its breath for 1 hour.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
entries: The hydra has five heads and is resistant to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and unconscious conditions. Whenever the hydra takes 25 or more damage in a single turn, one of its heads dies. If all its heads die, the hydra dies. At the end of its turn, it grows two heads for each of its heads that died since its last turn, unless it has taken fire damage since its last turn. The hydra regains 10 HP for each head regrown in this way.

trait.name: Reactive Heads
entries: For each head the hydra has beyond one, it gets an extra reaction that can be used only for opportunity attacks.

trait.name: Wakeful
entries: While the hydra sleeps, at least one of its heads is awake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hydra makes as many Bite attacks as it has heads.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) piercing damage.

action.name: Noxious Breath (Recharge Special)
entries: The hydra exhales poisonous gas in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 16 CON save, taking 40 (9d8) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. While the hydra has five or more heads, this action has a recharge of 6. The recharge range increases for each head the hydra has below five. For example, a hydra that starts its turn with two heads can recharge this feature on a 3, 4, 5, or 6.

bonus.name: Bloody Burst (Has Fewer Than Five Heads)
entries: The hydra squeezes the stumps of its severed heads, spraying blood and gore. Each creature within 5 feet of the hydra must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn and poisoned for 1 minute. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Infernal Champion A horned figure steps out of a burst of fire and brimstone, clad completely in armor. In one hand it holds a shield bearing the device of a grinning devil, in the other, a long, forked lance. The eyes in the helm glow bright and red.

bonus.name: Transformed Demons and Devils
entries: Infernal champions are fiends who have been elevated into a new form due to their prowess in battle. They are typically promoted by archdevils or demon lords, but balors and pit fiends can also promote a lesser fiend into a champion. These champions are dogged in their pursuit of their assigned tasks and only a true death in the Lower Planes stops them.

bonus.name: Loyal Servants
entries: Infernal champions swear an oath to serve their masters, or to fulfill specific tasks, and this oath is as binding as any devilish pact. They are unwaveringly loyal, whether they are commanded to eliminate a hated enemy or guard a valuable prize for centuries. Many demon lords chafe under this loyalty, mistrusting it or finding the unwavering infernal champion contradicting new goals by following older, oath-sworn orders. Because of this, most infernal champions are found serving archdevils rather than demon lords. Armed by Hell’s Forges. The armor infernal champions wear is made in forges heated by hellfire and cooled in mortal blood. The armor is then fused to the fiend’s flesh as part of its transformation into an infernal champion, permanently affixing the armor to the creature. The infamous forked lances they bear burn with hellfire when the champion is in battle.

trait.name: Fiendish Weapons
entries: The infernal champion’s weapon attacks are magical. When the champion hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 2d8 fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The infernal champion has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The infernal champion makes two Forked Lance attacks and one Shield Bash attack, or it makes four Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Forked Lance
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) fire damage.

action.name: Shield Bash
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) fire damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) fire damage.

reaction.name: Tail Lash
entries: When a creature moves into a space within 10 feet of the infernal champion, it can whip its armor-coated tail at the creature. The target must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or take 7 (2d6) slashing damage. Insatiable Brood The closely packed mass of tiny, winged heads dives down, the jagged teeth of each head gnashing hungrily.

reaction.name: Ravenous Swarm
entries: These ravenous beasts are always found in great, gnawing swarms. Each individual in an insatiable brood is a head, slightly larger than a human fist, with bulging black eyes swirling with Void energy and a large mouth lined with teeth. Wasp- like wings protrude from where the ears would be on a humanoid head. The sound of gnashing teeth precedes the brood as it travels.

reaction.name: Unceasing Appetite
entries: T rue to its name, an insatiable brood tries to devour everything it can reach, leaving a trail of devastation in its wake. Where the material a brood consumes actually goes is a mystery, as the individuals in a brood lack full bodies. Some sages speculate that the maws of these creatures are gateways to the Void. Others say they simply disintegrate what they ingest, exhaling it in clouds of dust. Killers of Life, Destroyers of Beauty. Although an insatiable brood is satisfied with devouring everything in its path, it prefers consuming living creatures first, with special attention to those trying to flee. When it senses no living creatures nearby, an insatiable brood devours examples of natural or artificial beauty, such as artwork or other finely crafted objects, reveling in the destruction of beauty.

monster.name: Insatiable Brood
languages: understands Void Speech but can’t speak

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The insatiable brood has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Bites
entries: The insatiable brood’s Bites attacks are magical.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The insatiable brood deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The insatiable brood can occupy another creature’s space and vice versa, and the brood can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny creature. The brood can’t regain HP or gain temporary HP .

trait.name: Swarm Resilience
entries: The swarm is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, and it is immune to the charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, and stunned conditions.

trait.name: Void Dweller
entries: The insatiable brood doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep, and it is immune to the effects of Void terrain. In addition, it can fly through the Void as if through air.

action.name: Bites
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 0 ft., one creature in the swarm’s space. Hit: 14 (4d6) piercing damage plus 18 (4d8) force damage, or 7 (2d6) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) force damage if the insatiable brood has half its HP or fewer.

bonus.name: Void Gaze
entries: The insatiable brood turns all its individual heads in one direction, and its hundreds of Void-filled eyes stare at one creature the brood can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its next turn. If the target fails the save by 5 or more, it suffers a random, short-term dread effect for 1 minute. Invisible Stalker Glowing eyes appear and vanish, leaving nothing behind but a slight breeze and a growing sense of dread.

bonus.name: Invisible Elementals
entries: Hailing from the Plane of Air, invisible stalkers are naturally transparent, elemental beings made up of living atmosphere. For those able to see invisible creatures, the invisible stalker is a vaguely humanoid cloud with yellowish, glowing eyes.

bonus.name: Compelled Servitors
entries: Long ago when mortals first learned to bind planar creatures into service, invisible stalkers were among the first. Mortals saw how the stalkers willingly served elemental lords and genies as spies and assassins and decided to use them for the same tasks. Invisible stalkers resent the magical compulsion—they had served elemental lords and genies willingly. Though they follow any order given by mortal spellcasters that have magically bound them, they always try to subvert those orders and long to break their binding and return home.

bonus.name: Bucolic Wanderers
entries: When encountered on the Plane of Air, invisible stalkers peacefully roam the plane, gaining sustenance by dining upon breezes and currents. Unfortunately, eons of forcible magical summons have left most invisible stalkers with a general distaste for mortals. They flee from any mortal encountered, attacking only if cornered or if they outnumber the mortals.

monster.name: Invisible Stalker
languages: understands Common but can’t speak it

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The invisible stalker doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Elemental Resilience
entries: The stalker is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Faultless Tracker
entries: At the end of each long rest, the invisible stalker can choose a creature it knows or has seen to be its quarry. A stalker summoned by another creature can choose only the quarry specified by its summoner, but the stalker also knows the location of its summoner while it continues to serve the summoner. The stalker always knows the direction and distance to its quarry, provided the two are on the same plane of existence.

trait.name: Invisibility
entries: The stalker is invisible. The condition’s effect on DEX (Stealth) checks has been included in the above Stealth value.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The stalker makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) cold damage. If the target is the stalker’s quarry, the target must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or its speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Steal Breath
entries: The invisible stalker calls on the air in the lungs of one breathing creature it can see within 30 feet of it, causing the air to leave the creature. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be unable to speak or cast spells with verbal components until the end of its next turn. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is also incapacitated and suffocating for the duration, as it coughs uncontrollably.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the kobold’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the kobold has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 3 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Dagger
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Sling
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Scurry
entries: The kobold moves up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If the kobold is aware of traps in the area, the kobold can choose if this movement triggers any of them. Kobolds Three short, draconic humanoids scurry out of the shadows, reptilian tails whipping back-and-forth with excitement. One grins mischievously while holding a contraption, another twirls a dagger with eyes alert to danger, and the third lights a candle on its miner’s cap.

bonus.name: Practical Minions
entries: Kobolds are diligent, hardworking creatures who view life through a practical lens. They often work beside larger, more powerful creatures, especially dragons, because it is more practical to work with such creatures than defy them and get eaten. Whether the larger creatures are dragons or humans, the relationship is typically mutually beneficial for both, and many kobold clans serve specific dragons or human family lines for generations.

bonus.name: Clever Miners
entries: Though kobolds can be found almost anywhere, they are most at home underground, in dark forests, or in the shadowed streets of close-built cities. Most are skilled miners and have at least a passing interest in alchemical substances or mechanical devices. Because of this, most kobold clan lairs are filled with traps.

bonus.name: Scaly Teamwork
entries: Kobolds share a strong communal instinct, knowing that only by acting together can they hope to defeat or survive threats against them. Y oung are hatched and raised in clutches, and many kobolds spend their entire lives close to their clutchmates, even if each pursues a different field. Kobolds without a clan or traveling the world often extend this mentality to their traveling companions, viewing even non-kobolds as their clutchmates.

bonus.name: Assassins and Alchemists
entries: Kobolds are quick studies and curious about anything and everything. This leads some kobolds to seek lives of excitement and new thrills as spies, assassins, and covert messengers, and it leads others to pursue lives of tinkering or witchcraft, where they create wondrous machines or strange potions.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the kobold’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the kobold has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 7 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold swiftblade makes two Scimitar or Hand Crossbow attacks.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

action.name: Hand Crossbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

bonus.name: Hidden Scurry
entries: The swiftblade moves up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks, and if it moves into a lightly or heavily obscured area, it takes the Hide action, using its Stealth value as the result of the check. If the swiftblade is aware of traps in the area, it can choose if this movement triggers any of them.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the kobold’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the kobold has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 5 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tinkerer makes two Tinkerer’s Hammer or Parts-Filled Sling attacks. Tinkerer’s Hammer. Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage. If the tinkerer makes this attack against a friendly Construct, the tinker has advantage on the attack roll, and the target regains HP instead of taking damage.

action.name: Parts-Filled Sling
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (the tinkerer’s choice).

action.name: Makeshift Ally (1/Day)
entries: The tinkerer cobbles together spare parts from its pockets and pouches, creating a Tiny Construct. The Construct can appear however the tinkerer wants, it obeys the tinkerer’s spoken commands, and it uses the statistics of a solodron mechadron. The Construct remains for 1 hour, until the tinkerer dies, or until the tinkerer dismisses it as a bonus action. When the Construct dies or is dismissed, it falls into its component parts.

bonus.name: Scurry
entries: The kobold moves up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If the kobold is aware of traps in the area, the kobold can choose if this movement triggers any of them.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
